races.race_name: British Stallion Studs EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Brunhilde
runners.spotlight: Brings solid claims having made the frame in both starts, keeping-on second at Salisbury (1m2f, good) latest; sets the form standard to boot and duly commands respect upped further in trip (bred to be suited by 1m4f).
runners.comment: Has made the frame in both starts, keeping-on second at Salisbury latest; the form pick

runners.name: Green Area
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to several winners, including two smart ones, for her stable; finished third of four at Lingfield (1m2f, good) where she was the sole newcomer and showed signs of greenness; should take a step forward.
runners.comment: Siblings include two smart winners for her stable; needed the experience in Lingfield race

runners.name: Wootton's Gal
runners.spotlight: Rallied for second of four, albeit 12l behind the overwhelming market leader, at Ripon (1m3f, good) on seasonal debut; bred to do better still, being from a family that has served her connections well.
runners.comment: Rallied for second, albeit 12l behind the winner, at Ripon on seasonal debut

runners.name: Orestina
runners.spotlight: 5yo mare who raced in two bumpers at Newcastle for Paul Robson, winning comfortably on AW then well beaten on turf; concedes a stone to some 3yos who have already shown promise under this code, on grass to boot.
runners.comment: Raced in two bumpers at Newcastle for Paul Robson, winning on AW then well held on turf

runners.name: Alpinara
runners.spotlight: 2,500,000gns yearling; sister to her yard's Arc winner Alpinista; only seventh of nine at Newbury (1m, soft) in sole 2yo start, having been described by her trainer beforehand as "more of a long-term prospect"; interesting on reappearance, with improvement seeming on the cards.
runners.comment: 2,500,000gns yearling; sister to her yard's Arc winner; should improve on 2yo effort

races.race_name: Talasey Restricted Novice Stakes (For Horses In Bands B, C And D) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Regional Rock
runners.spotlight: Absent and gelded since finishing 4l second to the market leader at Carlisle (6f, soft) a year ago when attempting to follow up his Windsor success (6f, good); sets the form standard on reappearance and, assuming all is fine, still has potential to boot.
runners.comment: Won at Windsor and second at Carlisle last term; gelded since; sets the form standard

runners.name: Sand Sea
runners.spotlight: Has kept on from rear in two races (6f/7f) over a year apart, showing a degree of promise; normally the type who should be more interesting in handicaps shortly but, on bare figures, has place possibilities in this field.
runners.comment: Has kept on from rear in two races; bare form gives him place possibilities

runners.name: Dark Angel Light
runners.spotlight: First foal of a 6f Listed winner; seemed to need the outing/experience in 5.7f contest at Bath, finishing seventh of 11; third best on bare figures in this field and is open to improvement; place claims.
runners.comment: Open to improvement with Bath effort under her belt; place claims

runners.name: Cullinan Wolf
runners.spotlight: 40,000gns foal, £48,000 yearling; Oasis Dream half-brother to 7f AW winner Puntarelle (RPR 79), out of Listed-placed 1m2f scorer (103); sole newcomer in the field and it's his belated debut; market instructive.
runners.comment: £48,000 yearling; by Oasis Dream; sole newcomer in the field; market instructive

runners.name: Auradue
runners.spotlight: 4yo filly who is getting familiar with Doncaster but isn't particularly solid on her two efforts, both here (1m/6f); likely to find better opportunities in handicaps soon.
runners.comment: Not particularly solid on her two efforts, both at Doncaster

runners.name: Elmfield
runners.spotlight: Finished fifth of ten in 6f AW maiden at Kempton where she was the sole newcomer in the field and proved easy to back; ran to a meagre RPR but may improve with the experience under her belt.
runners.comment: Ran to a meagre RPR in Kempton maiden but may improve

runners.name: Golden Harvest
runners.spotlight: Twice-raced 3yo who brings the worst from; drops a furlong in trip.
runners.comment: Brings the worst form

runners.name: Ronstadt
runners.spotlight: Never dangerous in 7f AW maiden at Kempton, having been weak in the betting, but may fare better with the outing under her belt; similar type to Elmfield.
runners.comment: May step up on Kempton effort; similar type to Elmfield
runners.owner: Mrs Bv Sangster,Justin Casse & Dr J Berk

runners.name: Zubaru
runners.spotlight: Progressive over 7f this term until latest start when admittedly not disgraced; won well at Doncaster to initiate a May double and he looks a possible danger if coping with the return to 6f.
runners.comment: 7f form this year includes a comfortable win at Doncaster; drops back in trip

runners.name: Photosynthesis
runners.spotlight: Ended last season with a win off just 1lb lower; has steadily found his form this term, creditable third over C&D most recently; possibilities, provided he takes well to first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Ran creditably over C&D last time; possibilities if taking well to headgear

runners.name: Winged Messenger
runners.spotlight: Not at best last time but is normally consistent and may rebound; gained most recent success in last year's Ayr Bronze Cup (by a neck off 2lb lower) and is 1-1 at Doncaster.
runners.comment: Not at best last time but is normally consistent and may rebound

runners.name: Ruby Red Gove
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who has a record of 2-4 since handicapping, most recently making all at Pontefract (6f, good) on turf debut; takes a step up in grade but warrants respect with further progress possible.
runners.comment: Record of 2-4 since handicapping; made all at Pontefract (turf debut) last time
runners.owner: Alfa Site Services & Alan Gove Racing

runners.name: Papa Cocktail
runners.spotlight: Tough sort whose form over C&D features a success last August and sound efforts in two of his last three outings; ties in with two of today's rivals on the latest one; solid chance.
runners.comment: Form over C&D includes sound efforts in two of last three starts; solid

runners.name: Alondra
runners.spotlight: Has done her winning over 5f on AW; behind a few of today's rivals over C&D most recently, taking turf record to 0-12; enough reasons to oppose her.
runners.comment: All wins at 5f on AW; below par over C&D most recently

runners.name: Sixtygeesbaby
runners.spotlight: Gave impression something was possibly amiss last time, taking record for new yard to 0-4; interesting on best form, which includes a win over C&D and close second to Photosynthesis at Newmarket.
runners.comment: 0-4 for new yard but is interesting on best form, which includes a C&D win

runners.name: Duran
runners.spotlight: Has posted duck eggs in big fields since his reappearance effort but this scenario may suit him better; performed well at Doncaster twice last term and he's 5lb below last winning mark.
runners.comment: Revival is possible in this scenario; 5lb below last winning mark
runners.owner: D Caslin, P Wilcock & M Cantillon

runners.name: Kats Bob
runners.spotlight: Very productive in the first half of 2025 but has subsequently been unable to add to his tally; admittedly ties in with a couple of today's opponents on C&D form three weeks ago (penultimate start).
runners.comment: Unable to add to his tally since a very productive first half of 2025

races.race_name: Edward Foster & Son Plumbers Merchant Bradford Handicap

runners.name: Magic Music
runners.spotlight: Has performed well over C&D (good to firm) in last two starts, finishing third then opening her account; this low-mileage 4yo may well improve further; respected kept to same scenario.
runners.comment: Opened her account in C&D event last time and may well improve further; respected

runners.name: Beale Street
runners.spotlight: Has done all his winning on Newcastle AW; however, finished a good second over C&D most recently and is due to go back up 1lb in future; holds a fighting chance in his attempt to go one better.
runners.comment: All wins on Newcastle AW; however, finished a good second over C&D most recently

runners.name: Al Muqdad
runners.spotlight: Productive spell in June/July 2024 featured a C&D success off 6lb higher; poor striike-rate since but he ran encouragingly at Ayr (first turf attempt of 2026) most recently; in the mix.
runners.comment: Ran encouragingly returned to turf last time; C&D winner off 6lb higher in 2024

runners.name: Al Hofzan
runners.spotlight: Has largely consistent form, close fourth at Leicester (7f) most recently; however, he's the only maiden in this field and chance of winning partly depends on the effects of added tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Consistent maiden but chance of winning depends on the effects of tongue-tie

runners.name: Shazani
runners.spotlight: Broadly inconsistent but his form at Doncaster features a success in last year's Leger Legends race and resurgent effort behind Bella Bisbee in latest start; better off with that rival and due to go back up 1lb in future.
runners.comment: Posted a resurgent effort behind Bella Bisbee over C&D last time; in the mix

runners.name: Enpassant
runners.spotlight: Has gained last three wins on AW, including latest start; effective on turf but has never won away from Leicester in this sphere; something to prove back up in grade.
runners.comment: Has gained last three wins on AW, including latest start; steps back up in grade

runners.name: Bella Bisbee
runners.spotlight: Belied her 20-1 odds with recent C&D success on good ground, taking Doncaster record to 2-5; incurs a penalty (future mark 1lb lower) but may well remain competitive kept to this venue.
runners.comment: Recent C&D success took her Doncaster record to 2-5; may well remain competitive

runners.name: Dingwall
runners.spotlight: Has never won above Class 6 and doesn't look as if he's about to put that right; consistent spell has been terminated by his form taking a dip in last few runs.
runners.comment: Consistent spell has been terminated by form taking a dip in last few runs

runners.name: Rizal
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish maiden who still has low mileage; did well to finish 1l third at Kempton (1m3f, AW; came from difficult position) on stable debut and sole start this year; interesting off same mark on return from another break.
runners.comment: Ex-Irish maiden; shaped well at Kempton on stable debut and sole run this year

runners.name: Superfortress
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden who has bits of encouraging form; good second, despite traffic issues, at Beverley (8.5f) on penultimate start and was possibly unsuited by Ripon latest; first win is not inconceivable.
runners.comment: Six-race maiden; has bits of encouraging form; perhaps best to forgive latest effort

runners.name: Lordsbridge Grey
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo has been kept to 1m since handicapping, running well on first occasion but disappointing in two starts this term; chance of a resurgence largely depends on the effects of new trip.
runners.comment: Chance of resurgence largely depends on the effects of new trip

runners.name: Stipulation
runners.spotlight: Generally consistent sort on turf/AW; looked better than bare result (met traffic issues) returned to this sphere last time; has frame possibilities, provided this race pans out better.
runners.comment: Generally consistent; looked better than bare result last time; frame possibilities
runners.owner: Horn And Trumpet Lads & S & A Mares

runners.name: Phaedra
runners.spotlight: Has gone close on AW a couple of times for new yard but failed to beat a rival last time; 3-7 in turf handicaps and looks quite interesting back in this sphere, off 1lb below last winning mark; tongue-tie fitted.
runners.comment: 3-7 in turf handicaps; interesting off an attractive mark back in this sphere

runners.name: Thequietman
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish maiden who has been essentially regressive for current stable; ended up tailed off (bled from nose) last time; perhaps best watched unless the market suggests a turnaround is likely.
runners.comment: Ex-Irish maiden; essentially regressive for current stable

runners.name: Finlaggan
runners.spotlight: Close second at Pontefract (1m2f) on handicap debut; hasn't made the expected progress in two starts since but ran creditably with cheekpieces applied at same venue latest; possibilities if added tongue-tie works a treat.
runners.comment: No progress the last twice but has possibilities if added tongue-tie works a treat

runners.name: Overbudget
runners.spotlight: Has reasonably consistent maiden/novice form; holds weaker claims on her two handicap efforts but this drop in grade may have positive effect; soft ground was a possible excuse last time.
runners.comment: Holds weak claims on her two handicap efforts; drops in grade
runners.owner: Matt Broughton & Hold My Beer Syndicate

runners.name: Black Endeavour
runners.spotlight: Steadily back to form this term, creditable third over C&D most recently; has slipped back to the mark off which his 2yo success was gained; this is a drop in class; therefore interesting on a few counts.
runners.comment: Creditable third over C&D last time; gained 2yo win off current mark; drops in class

runners.name: Twilight Serenade
runners.spotlight: Maiden who has inconsistent form overall and whose bare figures this season are meagre; receiving plenty of weight all round reflects the way he's been heading.
runners.comment: Has meagre bare figures this term

runners.name: Maximum Velocity
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent over 7f since switched to handicaps; prevailed at Redcar on penultimate start but isn't certain to repeat that form, especially as the return to 6f presents an added question mark.
runners.comment: Inconsistent over 7f since switched to handicaps, notwithstanding a recent win

runners.name: Pennine Way
runners.spotlight: Failed to get home over 7f on soft ground when making handicap debut last time; has shown promise otherwise and remains open to progress, being related to several winners (including one at Royal Ascot last week) for her connections; respected back down in trip.
runners.comment: Related to several winners for her connections; remains open to progress; respected

runners.name: Sprinting Speed
runners.spotlight: Ran quite encouragingly at Leicester in his only turf attempt since handicapping; interesting back in this sphere, assuming he responds well to first-time tongue-tie and cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Ran quite encouragingly in his only turf attempt since handicapping

runners.name: Beach Partee
runners.spotlight: Not particularly solid on his Class 5 form for current yard but this drop back in grade may have positive effect; recorded two of his three juvenile wins at this level.
runners.comment: May show the benefit of this drop back in grade

runners.name: Lamlash Bay
runners.spotlight: Well beaten on debut but has shown some ability with a couple of fourth-place finishes since; could go well on handicap debut, with Lewis Chalkley taking off a handy 7lb.
runners.comment: Could go well on handicap debut, with Lewis Chalkley taking off 7lb

runners.name: Come On Over
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden but proved resurgent with rallying fourth of 14 dropped to Class 6 over C&D most recently; possibilities off same mark in same grade, provided he builds on that effort.
runners.comment: Maiden who proved resurgent with rallying fourth of 14 over C&D most recently
runners.owner: Come On Over Syndicate & A H Yates Ltd

races.race_name: Download The Raceday Ready App Handicap

runners.name: La Tosca
runners.spotlight: Has shown positive signs in two starts since handicapping and switched to turf, finishing second both times, latest at Ayr (1m7f) a week ago; respected off same mark.
runners.comment: Runner-up in two races since handicapping and switched to turf, latest at Ayr; respected
runners.owner: Sheikh Hamdan bin Mohammed Al Maktoum

runners.name: Reem Rak
runners.spotlight: Flopped when favourite on handicap/seasonal debut but rebounded (good second over 1m4f) at Ffos Las since; possibilities off same mark upped further in trip.
runners.comment: Good second over 1m4f at Ffos Las most recently; possibilities upped further in trip

runners.name: Wardlaw
runners.spotlight: Prescott runner who has shown some improvement with a pair of second-place finishes since wearing headgear, latest at Sandown (1m6f, good); turn looks near.
runners.comment: Couple of second-place finishes since wearing headgear, latest at Sandown; turn looks near

runners.name: Mountbatten
runners.spotlight: Big improvement upped to 1m4f on handicap/seasonal debut when making all for an emphatic win at Southwell (1m4f, AW) last week; 4lb well in under a penalty for that success and he's open to more progress on this step up to 1m6f; should be hard to beat.
runners.comment: Emphatic win on handicap debut at Southwell (1m4f) and he's 4lb well in under a penalty

runners.name: Kelly Burn
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the sixth attempt with a narrow win in a Wolverhampton handicap (1m4f, AW) last month; disappointing favourite at Ffos Las (1m4f, good to soft) last time but he still has potential and has shaped as though this new trip should be within range; in the mix.
runners.comment: Disappointing favourite at Ffos Las but he still has potential and is not ruled out

runners.name: Queen Of Astolat
runners.spotlight: 0-5 but she ran her best race so far when a rallying third in handicap here (11.5, soft) two weeks ago; shaped like a stayer in that race and she could be dangerous if she can build on that.
runners.comment: Rallying third here (11.5f) two weeks ago and she shaped like a stayer in that race

runners.name: Leonato
runners.spotlight: His standout effort in three novice/maiden events was a 5l third at Lingfield (1m2f, AW) on his second start; not easy to weigh up on handicap debut but he's bred to be useful and his dam ran well in a Group 3 at this trip; needs a close look in the market.
runners.comment: Handicap newcomer and his dam ran well in a Group 3 at this trip; needs a close look
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

races.race_name: British Stallion Studs EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (EBF Restricted Race Qual') (GBB)

runners.name: Jazzy Blue
runners.spotlight: Ran green on her C&D debut last month but she left that form a long way behind when runner-up at Leicester (7f, good) 18 days ago; that was good effort under a prominent ride and she sets the standard on this drop back in trip; key player.
runners.comment: Big improvement when second at Leicester and she sets standard on that form; key player

runners.name: Rocsane
runners.spotlight: Made a low-key start over C&D last month but she took a major step forward when third at Lingfield (6f, AW) three weeks ago; that was a strong-finishing effort from off the pace and she's a player if she can build on that.
runners.comment: Promising third at Lingfield (6f, AW) and is a player if she can build on that

runners.name: Lady Rosalind
runners.spotlight: 38,000gns sister to 6f winner Soul Love (including 2yo/AW/Listed; RPR 100), half-sister to Group-placed 7f/1m winner Excellent Believe (112); dam Listed-placed 5f 2yo winner (96), out of half-sister to dam of good miler Mohaather; looks the part on paper and she's an interesting newcomer.
runners.comment: Sister to 6f Listed winner Soul Love; interesting newcomer and she needs a close look

runners.name: Zigazig Ah
runners.spotlight: February foal; Rajasinghe sister to two winners including Giant (7f/1m AW including 2yo; RPR 90); well-backed into 7-2 at Lingfield (6f, AW) this month and she made a bold bid before finishing a close second of ten (Last Dandelion third); that was a promising start and she's strongly respected.
runners.comment: Made a bold when runner-up on Lingfield debut and she's strongly respected

runners.name: Honeymoon Bear
runners.spotlight: 32,000gns yearling; fifth foal; half-sister to winners Dark Thirty (6f/7f including 2yo; RPR 103) and Sandy Craic (7f; 73); dam Listed-placed 7f-1m2f winner (including 2yo; 95), out of 1m4f/1m5f winning half-sister to 5f Listed winner Emerald Peace; first 2yo runner for yard this year and she's probably best watched.
runners.comment: 32,000gns yearling; first 2yo runner for yard this year and she's probably best watched

runners.name: Last Dandelion
runners.spotlight: Has improved with each of her three runs and was beaten less than 2l when third of ten at Lingfield (6f, AW) this month; finished behind Zigazig Ah in that race but she's on the right path and is not ruled out.
runners.comment: Has improved with each of her three starts and has each-way claims back on turf

runners.name: Consultation
runners.spotlight: Home-bred by Twilight Son; showed ability on Wolverhampton debut (6f, AW) last month and she built on that when third at Pontefract (6f, good); was no match for the first two last time but she has a good pedigree and should continue to improve with experience.
runners.comment: Has shown promise in both her runs and she should continue to improve with experience

runners.name: Crown Velocity
runners.spotlight: 20,000gns yearling; second foal; dam unraced sister to useful 6f 2yo/7f winner Maid A Million, half-sister to 7f Group 3 winner Dandhu, out of unraced half-sister to 1m Group 1 winner Rajeem; yard 15% with 2yos this season and she needs watching in market on debut; stablemate of Jazzy Blue.
runners.comment: 20,000gns yearling; yard 15% with 2yos this season and she needs watching in market

runners.name: Roberts Legacy
runners.spotlight: 18,000gns foal, 7,500gns yearling; second foal; dam unraced half-sister to French 9.5f winner Point Of Fact, out of French 1m 2yo winner, is granddaughter of French Oaks/Moulin winner Nebraska Tornado; has fair target to aim at on debut and she could be a longer-term prospect.
runners.comment: Has fair target to aim at on debut and she could be a longer-term prospect

runners.name: Decem Carolinae
runners.spotlight: £21,000 yearling; fourth foal; half-sister to 5f AW winner Sweet Carolina (RPR 74); dam 7f-8.6f winner (including AW Listed; 103), half-sister to useful 1m2f-1m6f Flat and 2m-2m4f hurdle/chase winner Totalize, family of Juddmonte winner One So Wonderful; has speed and stamina in pedigree and market should guide.
runners.comment: £21,000 yearling; has speed and stamina in pedigree and market should guide

runners.name: Miss U Gino
runners.spotlight: Beaten 7l in two turf runs (6f/5f, good) last month with RPRs in the 50s on both occasions; has plenty to find on the figures and nurseries will be more suitable.
runners.comment: Well held in two runs last month and has plenty to find on the figures

runners.name: Romidijo
runners.spotlight: Improved on her debut form when a rallying third at Chepstow (6f, good to soft) last month; found things tough at Epsom last time but that was in the Woodcote and she could resume her progress back in calmer waters here.
runners.comment: Found things tough in the Woodcote but she could resume her progress back in calmer waters

runners.name: Birkacre Brow
runners.spotlight: 9-1 for debut at Redcar (5f, good to firm) last month, when holding a prominent position before weakening; that form is modest and she needs lots of improvement upped in trip.
runners.comment: Well-held fourth at Redcar and she needs lots of improvement upped in trip

races.race_name: Winning Experience With Moulton Racing Handicap

runners.name: Luzon Heights
runners.spotlight: Won a Southwell handicap (1m, AW) last September and he's held his form with four creditable efforts since; still looks on a workable mark and has possibilities if this sets up for his closing style.
runners.comment: Hold-up performer who has a solid record in handicaps including a 1m win; respected

runners.name: Peel Park
runners.spotlight: Justified support in a Yarmouth maiden (6f, good to firm) last July; hasn't gone on from that in five subsequent runs but he was beaten less than 2l when fifth at Doncaster (7f; blinkers added) last time; that was eye-catching from off the pace and he's not ruled out back up in trip.
runners.comment: Back to form with an eye-catching effort at Doncaster (7f) and he's not ruled out

runners.name: Berry Clever
runners.spotlight: Just 1-13 on turf but that win came with a strong finish over 7f here (good) last month; held in fifth of 12 at Doncaster last time but that was a respectable effort and this step back up to 1m is no problem; in the mix.
runners.comment: Won over 7f here (good) last month and he's in the mix back up in trip

runners.name: Gorgeous Mr George
runners.spotlight: Yarmouth specialist who turned things around with a comfortable success over C&D (good to firm) last week; only 3lb higher under a penalty and he's a major player again in his bid for a fifth course win.
runners.comment: Yarmouth specialist who won over C&D last week and is only 3lb higher under a penalty

runners.name: Kaori
runners.spotlight: Made the frame in all three 2yo runs, but he started his handicap career with a remote last of six at Lingfield (1m2f, AW) this month; that was after 255 days off but he needs to bounce back with a personal best on this drop back in trip.
runners.comment: Unexposed filly but she flopped on her handicap/seasonal debut at Lingfield (1m2f, AW)
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: North View
runners.spotlight: Dual turf winner who scored on AW at Chelmsford (7f) in January but he's been well held back in this sphere in last two starts; now 2lb lower than for his last success but he needs to get back on track; cheekpieces reapplied.
runners.comment: On dangerous mark but he needs to get back on track with cheekpieces reapplied

runners.name: Distinct Spirit
runners.spotlight: Won on Salisbury debut (7f, good) in July 2024 but he's had only three runs since and he clipped heels and unseated his rider in his final run last season; hasn't progressed so far but he still has potential and needs watching in market on his return.
runners.comment: Very lightly raced 6yo and he needs watching in market on his return

runners.name: Daring Legend
runners.spotlight: Reappeared for new yard with a near-miss at Kempton (6f, AW) in March but he's been disappointing in three subsequent runs; wasn't beaten far over 7f here last month but he's untried at this trip and others are more convincing.
runners.comment: 5yo who is not easy to predict and he's untried at this trip; down the list

runners.name: Zafaan
runners.spotlight: Pulled up over hurdles on his reappearance last month but he's a dual Flat winner and his last success was in this race (good; 5-2 favourite) last year; yard is in good form and he's an interesting contender back in this sphere; cheekpieces reapplied.
runners.comment: Won this race last year and he's an interesting contender back on the Flat

runners.name: Raise The Stakes
runners.spotlight: 0-6 but he found improvement upped to 1m2f when runner-up in a classified event at Lingfield (good; first-time visor) last week; that was behind a rival who was completing a four-timer and he's a big player if he can build on that.
runners.comment: Improved form when second in a 1m2f classified at Lingfield (good) last week; respected

runners.name: Mist Of Time
runners.spotlight: Beaten 12l or more in all four runs including on handicap debut at Lingfield (1m, AW) three weeks ago; now steps up to 1m2f and she needs plenty of improvement; stablemate of Raise The Stakes.
runners.comment: Beaten 12l or more in all four starts and she needs improvement on this step up to 1m2f

runners.name: Ibiza Lights
runners.spotlight: Struggled in three qualifying runs and same story when tailed off on handicap debut here (1m, good to firm) in April; still early days but she needs a visor to make a big difference at this new trip.
runners.comment: Unexposed 3yo but she needs a visor to make a big difference at this new trip

runners.name: Hunting Berries
runners.spotlight: Tailed off at massive prices in three novice/maiden events at up to 1m2f; now drops into a classified event but she needs a transformation with cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Unexposed filly but she needs a transformation on this drop into a classified event

runners.name: Sunset In Paris
runners.spotlight: All six wins have come at around this trip on AW; pulled up back over hurdles last month and withdrawn in that code subsequently having been unruly at the post; best watched.
runners.comment: All six wins have been on AW and was pulled up over hurdles latest; best watched

runners.name: Rokuni
runners.spotlight: Sole win was in a maiden in August 2024 and he's struggled in two runs (7f/5.7f) for new yard this season; has lots to prove at this new trip and this looks tough on the figures.
runners.comment: Sole win was in 2024 and he's struggled in both runs for new yard; lots to prove

races.race_name: QuinnBet Best Odds Guaranteed "Confined" Handicap (For Horses Which Have Not Won In 2026)

runners.name: Kimbara
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 3yo who made a promising start in handicaps when a close second at Sandown (7f, good) two weeks ago; looks interesting off only 1lb higher here and he's open to more progress; key player.
runners.comment: Went close on his handicap debut at Sandown and he's open to more progress

runners.name: Raging Raj
runners.spotlight: Record of 2-4 on AW but he's not made an impact back on turf in his last three starts; on same mark as when third of six at Lingfield (7.6f, good to firm) last time and he has some work to do.
runners.comment: Dual AW winner but he's not made an impact back on turf in last three runs; work to do

runners.name: Mister Mojito
runners.spotlight: Three wins in the first half of 2025 and latest was off 1lb higher over C&D; returned after wind surgery with a promising effort in a Class 4 here (7f, good to firm) last month and he looks interesting on this drop back in grade.
runners.comment: C&D winner who returned after wind surgery with a promising run here last month; respected

runners.name: Generous Rascal
runners.spotlight: Kempton novice win (6f, AW) for Oliver Cole last October; placed in two of his three runs for new yard this season but he was beaten almost 5l when third at Kempton last time; edging down the weights but he needs to find more back up in trip.
runners.comment: Well held when third at Kempton last time and he needs to find more back up in trip

runners.name: Ecclefechan
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent 11-race maiden who produced one of his lesser efforts at Windsor (6f, good to firm) last time; this return to 7f is no problem but he needs to bounce back with cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Inconsistent 11-race maiden and he was well held at Windsor last time

runners.name: Exotic Baby
runners.spotlight: Won a Southwell maiden (1m, AW) last September but has mixed record in handicaps since and he was beaten 12l at Kempton (6f) last time; continues to drop down the weights but others are more convincing.
runners.comment: On reduced mark but he's now 1-8 and has a patchy profile; others are more convincing

runners.name: Rogue Allegiance
runners.spotlight: Sole win was on his debut in December 2024 but he's been knocking on door for Jack Jones this spring including when runner-up over C&D (good) last month; remains well treated on best form and he's a big player on this first run for James Owen; hood removed.
runners.comment: Close up over C&D in last two starts and he's a big player on first run for James Owen

runners.name: Red Hat Eagle
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped on his best form last season but last win was almost two years and he's finished down the field in his last four runs; now drops back in grade but this is competitive and others are more persuasive.
runners.comment: On dangerous mark but his last win was almost two years ago; down the list

races.race_name: QuinnBet Daily Free Bet Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Egotistical
runners.spotlight: Made it fourth-time lucky when landing a Redcar novice (6f, good to firm) in April but she flopped when favourite on handicap debut at Pontefract (seventh of eighth) three weeks ago; still has potential for top yard but she needs to bounce back after that last run.
runners.comment: Unexposed 3yo but she flopped at Pontefract last time and needs to bounce back

runners.name: Always Perfect
runners.spotlight: Showed promise in maiden/novice events and she made a solid start to her handicap career when third at Leicester (6f, good to firm) last month; nudged up 1lb but she gave it a good shot from the front last time and that form was boosted when the winner followed up under a penalty; interesting contender.
runners.comment: Good third behind subsequent winner on handicap debut at Leicester last month; interesting

runners.name: Cameley Days
runners.spotlight: Acts on any ground and she's a triple sprint winner on turf including over C&D; wasn't at her best at Goodwood three weeks ago but she made a bold bid at Lingfield on her penultimate run and has claims if she can recapture that form.
runners.comment: C&D winner who has been in good form this season including a Windsor win; dangerous

runners.name: Sahana
runners.spotlight: Added to her 1m win here (good to firm) last summer when scoring over 7f at Lingfield (AW) last September; only 2lb higher than for that win and she returned after six months off with a close second at Kempton (7f) last month; now drops to 6f for the first time but has claims if she gets a good pace.
runners.comment: Course winner who went close at Kempton (7f) latest; now drops to 6f but she's respected

runners.name: Fantasy Obsessor
runners.spotlight: Both wins (6f/7f) have been on AW but she was disappointing when favourite at Southwell (5f, Tapeta) last week; still unexposed on turf but she has form figures of 600 in this sphere and all of those runs were in handicaps; others preferred.
runners.comment: Dual AW winner but she was disappointing when favourite at Southwell latest; down the list

runners.name: Sir Rodneyredblood
runners.spotlight: 9yo who has won 16 times on AW; his sole turf win came at Yarmouth but he failed to beat a rival over C&D (good) when last seen in April and needs to bounce back after that 16l defeat; cheekpieces reapplied.
runners.comment: 16 of his 17 wins have been on AW and he failed to beat a rival over C&D last time

runners.name: Coolagh Magic
runners.spotlight: Nine-time AW winner who is 0-23 on turf, but he's versatile ground-wise and has finished runner-up in two of his five starts at Yarmouth; hasn't threatened back on turf in last two runs but he ran respectably over C&D last time and he's in the mix back in a classified event.
runners.comment: 0-23 on turf but he's finished runner-up in two of his five runs at Yarmouth; in the mix
runners.owner: Lady Emma Oshea And A Carson

runners.name: Shining Guest
runners.spotlight: Beat only one rival in three maiden/novice events (7f/6f, good) but it's still early days for this well-bred filly and she's a possible improver now dropped into a 0-50 event; needs a close look in the market.
runners.comment: 3yo filly and a possible improver dropped into a classified event; needs close look

runners.name: Valadero
runners.spotlight: Has claims on a couple of runner-up efforts over 6f (good to firm) last summer but he's still a maiden after 31 starts and was well held in classified events in his final two runs last winter; now returns to sprinting but he needs to revive after 165 days off.
runners.comment: 31-race maiden and he needs to revive back at 6f on his reappearance

runners.name: Bear Steps
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 3yo but she's not finished closer than 8l to a winner and was last of 14 in a handicap here (7f, soft) two weeks ago; has plenty to prove and she needs to show a lot more on this drop to 6f.
runners.comment: Unexposed 3yo but she needs a transformation on this drop to 6f

runners.name: Wondrous
runners.spotlight: Struggled in three qualifying runs last year and same story when tailed-off on handicap debut at Ripon (1m, good) last month; may have needed that run but she needs a transformation on this return to 6f.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 3yo but she needs lots of improvement on this return to 6f

races.race_name: RF Miller & Co Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Loch Cuan
runners.spotlight: Beaten 25l and 9l when runner-up in his first two maiden hurdles before readily justifying favouritism under Sean Bowen over C&D (good) a month ago; penalised accordingly but looks very capable of following up.
runners.comment: Ready winner under Sean Bowen here a month ago; every chance of following up

runners.name: Dream Racer
runners.spotlight: Bought for £6,000 after finishing 21l off the winner in a 3m good-ground Irish point last month; she's an unlikely winner judged on that performance.
runners.comment: Bought for £6,000 after finishing 21l off the winner in a 3m good-ground Irish point

runners.name: Manhatane
runners.spotlight: Insufficient promise in points to make any great appeal, for all that she's a 4yo filly with the allowances and with a 10lb claimer up top to boot.
runners.comment: Insufficient promise in points to make any great appeal

runners.name: Tribal Moon
runners.spotlight: Two 1m wins at Dundalk for his previous yard; only seventh of 12 in a 2m6f maiden at Downpatrick but the RPR he recorded that day firmly puts him in the mix.
runners.comment: Dual AW winner; latest RPR in a 2m6f maiden firmly puts him in the mix

runners.name: Drumconnor Hill
runners.spotlight: Three ran when second in a point; his latest fourth of 17 at Ballinrobe (2m, good) offered something for the future and he's raised in distance here, which should benefit.
runners.comment: Latest fourth of 17 at Ballinrobe (2m, good) offered something for the future

runners.name: God Help Me
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in a point and his fourth of 18 in a 2m maiden hurdle at Navan (soft) in March 2025 was a promising effort; however, confined to only two runs since and has struggled; sights are lowered here on first run since September; wouldn't be too dismissive.
runners.comment: Sights are lowered here on first run since September; wouldn't be too dismissive

runners.name: Parish Star
runners.spotlight: 2-6 for Adam Nichol and was only just denied off 3lb lower at Market Rasen a month ago (2m4f, good); in form and should stay this far.
runners.comment: 2-6 for Adam Nichol and was only just denied off 3lb lower at Market Rasen a month ago

runners.name: Alvesta
runners.spotlight: Having her 32nd career start when finally off the mark in a selling handicap over 2m1f here recently (good); only 4lb higher and new trainer James Moffatt has booked Brian Hughes; has to be of interest despite blatant stamina reservations.
runners.comment: Belatedly off the mark in a selling handicap here recently; up 4lb but also up in trip

runners.name: Blackwater Lilly
runners.spotlight: Has the form to get in contention but she had a shocker when tailed off in a first-time visor at Fakenham; regularly worn cheekpieces but now goes without any headgear.
runners.comment: Had a shocker last time in a new visor; often wears cheekpieces but nothing here

runners.name: Gerrys Wish
runners.spotlight: Ten-race maiden who threatened on occasions last year, going close over 3m at Perth and Galway; tailed off in May after 199 days off but could be a different proposition this time.
runners.comment: Tailed off in May after 199 days off but could be a different proposition this time

runners.name: Masonbrook Meadow
runners.spotlight: Runner-up over 2m7f at Fakenham last month, since when she's finished 12l behind at Stratford; only 1-33 overall and others preferred for win purposes.
runners.comment: Only 1-33 overall and others preferred for win purposes

runners.name: Forest Spirit
runners.spotlight: Dual AW winner; 0-6 over hurdles; in first-time cheekpieces when runner-up over 2m4f at Ffos Las (good to soft; fine on good) and she wasn't stopping; interesting now sent further.
runners.comment: Runner-up over 2m4f at Ffos Las (good to soft; fine on good) and she wasn't stopping

runners.name: Turkey And Ham
runners.spotlight: Won a 2m2f handicap at Kilbeggan in April 2025 (good to yielding) and didn't run shockingly after a year out at Tramore two months ago; cheekpieces are turned to and this Irish challenger could go well.
runners.comment: Didn't run shockingly after a year out at Tramore two months ago; cheekpieces added

runners.name: Fair Damsel
runners.spotlight: Won an Irish maiden in 2024; ran well for a 66-1 chance to be beaten under 8l at Wexford last time (2m4f, good to yielding) but with top weight it's probably best to turn elsewhere.
runners.comment: Ran well for a 66-1 chance to be beaten under 8l at Wexford last time

runners.name: Victoria Milano
runners.spotlight: Dual novice winner for previous yard; building up some momentum for this one after beating all bar an improver at Bangor (2m7f, good); considered.
runners.comment: Building up some momentum for this yard after beating all bar an improver at Bangor

runners.name: Belles Benefit
runners.spotlight: Won this two years ago after a 157-day absence when 3lb lower and has won two more since; she wasn't in great form when last seen but could easily stage a revival.
runners.comment: Previous winner of this race and could easily stage a revival on the back of a break

races.race_name: Molson Coors Novices' Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Passengerontheship
runners.spotlight: The tongue-tie has made a difference and he completed a Hexham four-timer five days ago (2m4f, good); only two others got round but he's on a roll and might be well in with a penalty.
runners.comment: On a five-timer; only two others got round last time but he's very much on a roll

runners.name: Settle Down Jill
runners.spotlight: Comfortable winner of a Kelso hunter chase (2m7f, good) last month but was beaten fair and square by about 10l in a 3m handicap at Perth since and has only dropped 1lb.
runners.comment: Beaten about 10l in a 3m handicap at Perth last time and has only dropped 1lb

runners.name: Back In The Bay
runners.spotlight: Second hurdle win for this yard when taking a mares' handicap at Stratford (2m3f, good) last July but she's been very disappointing in her last two runs after lengthy breaks; makes chase debut and she was second in a point.
runners.comment: Capable hurdler but has finished tailed off in her last two races; chase debut
runners.owner: Value Racing - Back In The Bay

runners.name: Jack Sprat
runners.spotlight: Capable on his day for Jamie Snowden, winning three times; didn't sign off for that yard in bad form but he made a quiet debut for this yard here when 3m1f was no excuse.
runners.comment: Three wins but he made a quiet debut for this yard here when 3m1f was no excuse

runners.name: Justicialism
runners.spotlight: Fell and tailed off in his two previous chases in late 2023; lightly raced in the meantime but returns to this discipline after three solid runs over hurdles, all handicaps from 2m1f to 2m4f; likely to go well again if he still fancies fences.
runners.comment: In good form over hurdles; this is a belated return to fences but he could go well

runners.name: Aslukgoes
runners.spotlight: Three bumper wins included Listed honours and he won a maiden hurdle last summer; goes chasing on the back of a close fourth at Ffos Las (2m4f, good to soft; perhaps better on good ground) and should have a role to play if finding some fluency.
runners.comment: Goes chasing on the back of a good run and he'll enjoy the ground; chance

races.race_name: Lakes Luxury Loo's Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Stratagem
runners.spotlight: Has looked in good nick since returning from wind surgery; however, he was close up behind Caughtinyourtrance here last month and he's 8lb higher now after winning a Class 3 handicap over 3m5f here two days later by 7.5l (Fairlawn Flyer third); he's still a major player with James Murray, aboard last time, again up top.
runners.comment: Won a Class 3 here last month by 7.5l but the 8lb rise asks a question of him

runners.name: Fairlawn Flyer
runners.spotlight: Admirable strike-rate of 10-29 over fences; won this 12 months ago off 1lb higher and had only five starts in the meantime, the latest respectable in a higher grade behind stablemate Stratagem; chance.
runners.comment: Last year's winner and latest third here was respectable given the higher grade

runners.name: Caughtinyourtrance
runners.spotlight: Won twice over hurdles at Cartmel in 2025; following a wind operation, he's finished runner-up in 3m1f/2m5f handicap chases here, last time only reeled in late on; clear claims off an untouched mark.
runners.comment: Second in both races after wind surgery and returns here off an untouched mark

runners.name: Ivane
runners.spotlight: Won over hurdles and fences last season but that campaign ended on a low note and he finished well behind after a break at Stratford; even if back on song this mark is fairly tough.
runners.comment: Below par last three races; even if back on song this mark is fairly tough

runners.name: Le Grand Vert
runners.spotlight: None too consistent but he was back over fences at Punchestown 20 days ago (3m1f, good to yielding) and ran on well to prevail in a big field; that was his fourth success as a chaser and big shout if he can hold his form.
runners.comment: Beat a big field at Punchestown 20 days ago and big shout if he can hold his form

runners.name: Tiger Orchid
runners.spotlight: All good since joining Stuart Edmonds and deserved his 2m7f win over hurdles at Uttoxeter (by 4l; good); up 4lb for this return to fences but he's a three-time winner in this discipline.
runners.comment: Deserved his 2m7f win over hurdles at Uttoxeter; up 4lb; three-time chase winner

runners.name: Red Happy
runners.spotlight: Completed a hat-trick at around 3m during the opening quarter of 2025 but following a year out he refused to race on consecutive appearances this spring.
runners.comment: Capable chaser but following a year out he refused to race twice this spring

runners.name: Zumbi
runners.spotlight: First win for a long time and over fences when it was never in much doubt here a month ago (2m1f, good; first-time cheekpieces); went up 8lb but Charlotte Jones announced her retirement straight after that race so 10lb claimer Leah Noreci now takes over; weighted to go well again and this longer trip won't trouble him.
runners.comment: Never in much doubt here a month ago and weighted to go well again; stays this far

runners.name: Judicial Review
runners.spotlight: Three-time course winner but he's had his off days here as well, including a C&D effort last month; he's still hard to dismiss off a perfectly good mark.
runners.comment: Three-time course winner but he's had his off days here as well, including last month

runners.name: Out On Her Own
runners.spotlight: Ran some fair races in defeat over hurdles and a similar story in two runs over fences in races won by the improving Passengerontheship who runs earlier on this card; hood fitted for the penultimate run returns; might not be far away.
runners.comment: Two fair runs over fences behind in-form Passengerontheship who runs earlier on this card

runners.name: Am Still Here
runners.spotlight: Went 0-15 for Nicky Richards but was runner-up in four of his six chases and he had form here; could be interesting back from a break for his new yard, and Sean Quinlan knows him well.
runners.comment: Went 0-15 for Nicky Richards but was runner-up in four of his six chases and had form here

runners.name: Thank You Blue
runners.spotlight: Won three on the bounce over hurdles in February (2m-2m4f, good to soft/soft) but has lost his form in the blink of an eye and finished last on his recent return to chasing (0-8).
runners.comment: Has lost his form in the blink of an eye and finished last on his recent return to chasing

runners.name: Roi Du Roume
runners.spotlight: 0-5 over hurdles; won an Irish point so no surprise to see him run well for a long way on chase debut at Newton Abbot (2m5f, good); he was beaten about 17l in the end and two new forms of headgear are now enlisted, but couldn't rule out.
runners.comment: Irish point winner so no surprise to see him run well for a long way on chase debut

runners.name: Mr Globalist
runners.spotlight: Longstanding maiden who often races freely and he's 3lb out of the weights for this second run over fences; he was well held in the first.
runners.comment: Often races freely and he's 3lb out of the weights for this second run over fences

runners.name: Blue Reed
runners.spotlight: Hasn't offered a lot in four previous runs over fences, the latest here 12 months ago; however, has another crack at this game after winning over hurdles at Kilbeggan last month (2m3f, good to yielding); has to be feared.
runners.comment: Has another crack at this game after winning over hurdles at Kilbeggan

runners.name: Tell Me Again
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish ten-race maiden who is also 0-4 in points; tailed off in his two completed starts for this yard, finishing well behind Out On Her Own in one of those.
runners.comment: Ex-Irish ten-race maiden; tailed off in his two completed starts for this yard

races.race_name: Cartmel Priory Cofe School Amateur Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Dusky Days
runners.spotlight: Not beaten all that far in 2m6f/3m1f handicaps on last two starts but he was a weakener late on; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Not beaten all that far in 2m6f/3m1f handicaps on last two starts but he was a weakener

runners.name: Zamond
runners.spotlight: He has competitive form here but not last month when tailed off in a selling handicap; that was after 167 days away but he had to show more.
runners.comment: He has competitive form here but not last month when tailed off in a selling handicap

runners.name: Dog Fox
runners.spotlight: Won twice on the Flat and over hurdles; had been chasing before beaten 9l in a Bangor handicap hurdle (2m, good) and that was after 173 days away; on last winning mark if he can find something on that form.
runners.comment: Fair return to hurdles at Bangor and he lurks on his last winning mark
runners.owner: The North West National Hunt Racing Club

runners.name: Goodwin Face
runners.spotlight: Second run back from an absence when scoring comfortably at Plumpton (2m, good) to earn this 8lb higher mark; hasn't had a lot of racing and he's Chris Gordon's only runner on today's card.
runners.comment: Second run back from an absence when scoring comfortably at Plumpton; raised 8lb

runners.name: Book Of Secrets
runners.spotlight: Hard to win with (2-31) but he was only a neck away in this last year off 5lb higher when ridden by Natasha Cookson, and he also went close in September; needs to get back to that level, though.
runners.comment: Nearly won this last year off 5lb higher but he needs to get back to that level

runners.name: Jolie Coeur Allen
runners.spotlight: Only 1-11 over hurdles and the win came in a maiden; however, this mark shouldn't be beyond her and she was quite free after a break when beaten 13l into third at Sedgefield (2m1f, good); has form here.
runners.comment: She was quite free after a break when beaten 13l into third at Sedgefield

runners.name: Lightening Company
runners.spotlight: Has been running on the Flat, winning 1m4f/1m2f handicaps before a respectable effort in a competitive race at York; capable hurdler with winning form here and James Murray takes off a useful 7lb.
runners.comment: Has been running well on the Flat; capable hurdler with winning form here
runners.owner: Middleham Park Cxv, Lycett Rcg & Partner

runners.name: Garrick Painter
runners.spotlight: 10lb higher than for his Perth win last June (2m, good), after which he ran well on the Flat at Galway; back from 289 days off when beaten about 12l at Wexford and that could have been needed; on a tough mark, though.
runners.comment: Beaten about 12l at Wexford and that could have been needed; on a tough mark, though

runners.name: Out Out
runners.spotlight: Didn't last long with the Skeltons, though he was second in a bumper and in a handicap hurdle; last seen winning a match between the flags in May 2025; needs checking in the betting on first run for Neil King.
runners.comment: Placed under rules; won a match in a point when last seen over a year ago; check betting

runners.name: Envious Editor
runners.spotlight: Useful hunter chase form was a long time ago, and he was unable to win any of his last six points; very opposable returned to hurdles, especially at this trip.
runners.comment: Unable to win any of his last six points; very opposable returned to hurdles

races.race_name: JenningsBet In Newcastle Amateur Jockeys' Handicap

runners.name: Royal Blaze
runners.spotlight: Both wins last season were over 1m2f, latterly over C&D in September off 1lb lower than today; two minor honours from three starts this turf season ensure that he has a prominent place in calculations.
runners.comment: C&D winner last September; two minor honours from three starts this turf season

runners.name: Wicklow Way
runners.spotlight: 1m2f classified event here in March was his only win from 12 races; returned to some form when third at Beverley (8.5f) on latest start; return to 1m2f should suit if this is another of his good days.
runners.comment: 1m2f classified here in March was his only win (12 runs); returned to some form on latest

runners.name: Maple
runners.spotlight: Had a hurdles win in spring 2025 and 2026; 0-9 on Flat but two C&D seconds early this year put him in the picture.
runners.comment: Had a hurdles win in spring 2025 and 2026; 0-9 on Flat but two C&D seconds early this year
runners.owner: R Whitelock, L Clemitson & R Jefferson

runners.name: The Pug
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent for three previous yards, winning just once (last August) since 2023; satisfactory debut for new trainer when third at Chepstow (1m2f) 11 days ago, however, and probably effective here, so needs a second look.
runners.comment: Inconsistent for three previous yards; satisfactory debut for new trainer 11 days ago

runners.name: The Ubermensch
runners.spotlight: 0-8 but he's finished runner-up twice and wasn't far away on last two outings either (upped to 1m4f latest); tongue tied on last two, with cheekpieces as well on latest; acts on Tapeta; probably thereabouts.
runners.comment: 0-8 but he's finished runner-up twice and wasn't far away on last two outings

runners.name: Inspiring Speeches
runners.spotlight: Back-to-back wins last summer in six-runner turf handicaps over 1m2f/1m4f; back to form on last two outings, which suggests an each-way chance despite the patchy record in his five AW outings.
runners.comment: Back to form on last two outings; e-w chance despite patchy record in his five AW outings

runners.name: Kitsune Power
runners.spotlight: No win since June 2024 off 15lb higher and he's never made much of an impact in his five AW races; he's still shown more than enough on turf this season to be considered, however.
runners.comment: Not much impact in his five AW races; considered on what he's shown on turf this season

runners.name: He's Our Cracker
runners.spotlight: Cheekpieces seemed to have a positive effect early in the year, notably for a 1m2f win on Chelmsford Polytrack, but he was soundly beaten in them on penultimate start and below form when hooded on latest; now he has cheekpieces plus a first-time tongue-tie; lightly raced but none too solid.
runners.comment: 1m2f win on Chelmsford Polytrack; first-time tongue-tie; lightly raced but none too solid

runners.name: Glitter Code
runners.spotlight: Second five times at up to 1m2f, including in first blinkers at Beverley in April on yard debut; heavy defeat over 1m4f four weeks ago sees a visor return today; 0-21 tally (0-5 on AW) is probably the bottom line.
runners.comment: Second five times at up to 1m2f but his 0-21 tally (0-5 on AW) is probably the bottom line

runners.name: Luan
runners.spotlight: Closest finish when beaten 6l on latest start but still managed only ninth of 11 in that 9.4f handicap at Wolverhampon (Tapeta; just 7-1) when tongue tied first time, after 231 days off; lightly raced but lacks solid claims.
runners.comment: Closest finish when beaten 6l on latest start but still managed only ninth of 11

runners.name: Yurinov
runners.spotlight: 28-race maiden; second on Southwell Tapeta (1m6f) last October and bits of form since, but others have to be preferred.
runners.comment: 28-race maiden; second on Southwell Tapeta (1m6f) last October and bits of form since

runners.name: Beat The Odds
runners.spotlight: Triple-figure prices for all four starts, the latest her handicap debut at Wolverhampton (9.4f, AW; made most) when finishing a lot closer but in only eighth of 11; no form claims.
runners.comment: Triple-figure prices for all four starts, the latest her handicap debut; no form claims

runners.name: Ivynator
runners.spotlight: Latest of his two wins was in 2021; runner-up over hurdles last May but very opposable on what he's done since; off 260 days before latest start when he was remote at 200-1; first-time headgear this evening.
runners.comment: Off 260 days before remote at 200-1 for latest start; first-time headgear this evening

runners.name: Langton Wold
runners.spotlight: Not seen since runner-up over fences in December 2024 and his light AW record is less convincing than that on turf.
runners.comment: Not seen since runner-up over fences in December 2024; light AW record is not convincing
runners.owner: Brian Ellison Racing Club & M Dale

races.race_name: JenningsBet In Shiremoor Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Binmalk
runners.spotlight: Left last month's debut run behind when fourth of 11 at Doncaster (7f, good to firm); his illustrious pedigree provides optimism that he will continue to progress and he holds leading claims.
runners.comment: Promising 7f fourth on second start and can continue to progress; leading claims

runners.name: Viper
runners.spotlight: Close third over C&D last month on second start and another good run when second at Pontefract (6f, good to soft) last time, making the running on each occasion; solid claims.
runners.comment: Placed on last two starts (C&D on first occasion) and he holds solid claims

runners.name: Leopards Rock
runners.spotlight: 33-1 and slowly away on last month's debut at Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW), but his 5l third of six was an encouraging effort and the runner-up won next time; could be sharper for that initial experience; possible player.
runners.comment: Encouraging third on last month's debut and could be sharper for that experience; chance

runners.name: Back At One
runners.spotlight: Always towards the rear on last month's debut at Kempton (7f, AW; 10-1) and beaten 8l when sixth of 13 at 66-1 at Salisbury (7f, soft) last time; plenty of improvement is needed but this 160,000euros yearling is from a respected Newmarket stable and could take a big step forward at some point.
runners.comment: Well beaten on both runs but from good yard and could take big step forward at some point

runners.name: Jetpack Jimmy
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; Masar half-brother to 1m2f winner Tiberio Force (RPR 60); dam 1m2f/1m4f winner (95) from family of Irish Oaks winner Bolas; it seems likely this newcomer will come into his own over further.
runners.comment: Masar half-brother to 1m2f winner (RPR 60); likely to come into his own over further

runners.name: Shadow Road
runners.spotlight: Seventh foal; Lope De Vega brother to winners Duke De Sessa (1m-1m4f including Australian Group 1), Walsingham (1m; RPR 95) and Newtown Duke (1m AW; 84), half-brother to two winners; makes debut with yard in fine form and he's one to consider, especially if strong in the betting.
runners.comment: Brother to 1m4f Australian Group 1 winner; makes debut with yard in fine form

runners.name: Lineofsuccession
runners.spotlight: Not devoid of promise when keeping on for sixth of eight on last month's debut at Pontefract (6f, good to soft) but well beaten at 200-1 at Doncaster (7f, good to firm) since and others are preferred.
runners.comment: Not devoid of promise on last month's debut but well beaten at 200-1 since

runners.name: Rake Lane
runners.spotlight: 37,000gns foal, 20,000gns yearling; second foal; brother to 7f AW/1m2f turf winner Titainium (RPR 77); dam unraced half-sister to two classy winners; refused to enter stalls and withdrawn from this month's intended debut at Thirsk (40-1 at the time); probably best watched.
runners.comment: Brother to 7f AW/1m2f turf winner (RPR 77); 40-1 when withdrawn from intended debut

runners.name: Hawksworth
runners.spotlight: Well beaten at 25-1 then 100-1 this month on his first two starts (1m2f/7f, good to firm) and a transformation is needed.
runners.comment: Well beaten at 25-1 then 100-1 this month on his first two starts; transformation needed

runners.name: Real Trouper
runners.spotlight: 28-1 when soundly beaten third of five at 28-1 on debut in a warm novice at York (1m, good to firm) last May; he's been absent since but that was an encouraging start to his career and he's not ruled out on his return.
runners.comment: Encouraging start to career at York last May; absent since but not ruled out on return
runners.owner: Peter Onslow, FDC Holdings Ltd & NWIR Ltd

runners.name: Heathers Feather
runners.spotlight: Closely related to 7f 2yo winner Courageous Knight (RPR 94) but beaten a long way at triple-figure odds on his first two starts.
runners.comment: Beaten a long way at triple-figure odds on his first two starts

runners.name: Callianassa
runners.spotlight: Three-time C&D winner, including this race last year, who ran well when seventh of 24 in the Kensington Palace at Royal Ascot last week; she's below her last winning mark and has strong claims back down in grade.
runners.comment: Won this last year and ran well at Royal Ascot last week; strong claims back down in grade

runners.name: Jazzy Baby
runners.spotlight: Won Kempton maiden (1m, Polytrack) in January on fourth start and initially continued in good form in handicaps there; well beaten at Kempton (1m) then Salisbury (1m2f, soft) on last two starts; the return to AW and drop back in trip may help but she has something to prove.
runners.comment: Made good start to life in handicaps in February/March but well beaten on last two starts

runners.name: Montezin
runners.spotlight: Won 6f maiden here last November and this lightly raced 4yo has got closer to the target with each of his three turf handicap runs, keeping on for fourth at Doncaster (7f) last time in first-time cheekpieces (retained); the return to Newcastle could be a plus and the step up to 1m is worth a go.
runners.comment: Lightly raced 4yo who won his maiden here; the return to Newcastle could be a plus

runners.name: Chuzzlewit
runners.spotlight: He's 9lb lower than when third in this last year and he returned to form when going close over C&D early this month; has a modest strike-rate but he can be bang there if backing up that latest performance.
runners.comment: Modest strike-rate but third in this last year and runner-up over C&D most recently

runners.name: Kanishka
runners.spotlight: Won 7f 2yo maiden here last June on sole previous course visit and running well in defeat in handicaps this year, most recently third at Carlisle (1m, good to firm) when making the running; he's one to consider.
runners.comment: Won 7f 2yo maiden last June on sole previous course visit; third at Carlisle most recently
runners.owner: C Booth, P Boyle, S Coleby & S Smith

runners.name: Harry The Rogue
runners.spotlight: Went close over C&D in March on stable debut and another good run when fourth at Musselburgh (1m) next time; below par at York on his last two starts but he tackled 1m2f last time and is not ruled out back down to 1m.
runners.comment: Went close over C&D in March on stable debut and he's not ruled out back down to 1m

runners.name: Sailthisshipalone
runners.spotlight: Won on stable/seasonal debut at Pontefract (1m, good to soft) and he's remained in good heart on both starts since (1m, good/good to firm); each-way claims provided the return to AW isn't an issue.
runners.comment: Won on stable debut at Pontefract and he's remained in good heart; each-way claims

runners.name: Asian Journey
runners.spotlight: Had been expensive to follow but he got off the mark at the 17th attempt at Wolverhampton (8.6f, Tapeta) this month and has since been runner-up there (9.4f); also effective here and he's an each-way player.
runners.comment: Got off the mark at Wolverhampton this month; second there since; each-way player

runners.name: Victory Ace
runners.spotlight: 3yo who won on handicap/seasonal debut at Lingfield (7f, Polytrack) in April and good second at Kempton (1m, Polytrack) next time; disappointing at Musselburgh three weeks ago but could leave that behind back on AW; well worth a second look with yard in excellent form.
runners.comment: Strong AW handicap form this year and return to turf may not have suited at Musselburgh

runners.name: Bowood
runners.spotlight: He hasn't been at his very best this year and a refusal to settle is becoming an issue; however, he was returning from a break at Doncaster three weeks ago and that run may have got the freshness out of him; impressive when winning two in a row over C&D last autumn and not written off.
runners.comment: Refusal to settle is becoming an issue but he twice impressed over C&D last autumn

runners.name: Intrusively
runners.spotlight: Down the field on three of his four starts for this yard, including at Doncaster three weeks ago on latest outing; however, prior to that he caught the eye with a close fourth at Nottingham and he's 4lb lower today; capable of going well.
runners.comment: Just the one good run this year, from four attempts, but capable of going well if on song

runners.name: They All Know Me
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the ninth attempt at Carlisle (7f, soft) last month; soundly beaten at Doncaster (7f, good) since but it's possible he'll be able to bounce back; has run well on Tapeta elsewhere.
runners.comment: Won at Carlisle last month; soundly beaten since but may be able to bounce back

runners.name: Stanage
runners.spotlight: He's 9lb lower than when winning at Doncaster last March and may have needed his comeback run there this March, when mid-division; however, his form went the wrong way as last year developed and he needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: He's 9lb below his last winning mark but needs to get back on track

runners.name: Racingbreaks Ryder
runners.spotlight: Drew a blank during regressive 2025 campaign and down the field on reappearance at Chepstow three weeks ago; however, he left last season's comeback run well behind to post 33-1 third on his next start, and he's not discounted off a reduced mark.
runners.comment: Regressive in 2025 but on a reduced mark and could leave his reappearance run well behind
runners.owner: Cottier, Racing Breaks & Old Gold Racing

races.race_name: JenningsBet In Delves Hoppings Fillies' Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies & Mares)

runners.name: Diamond Rain
runners.spotlight: Won this last year prior to finishing placed in two North American Grade 1s; not at her best when third of fourth in Group 2 at York on last month's reappearance but it wasn't a bad run and she may well have needed it; very much the one to beat if reproducing anything close to her peak form.
runners.comment: Won this last year then placed in two North American Grade 1s; she's the one to beat

runners.name: Botagoz
runners.spotlight: Novice wins on first two starts (7f/1m, AW/good) before keeping on strongly for Listed third at York (1m, good) in May; a sizeable step forward is needed today but the extra yardage could be a firm plus; one to consider.
runners.comment: 2-2 prior to Listed third over 1m at York; the step up in trip could be a firm plus

runners.name: Dreamasar
runners.spotlight: 4yo who has raced only five times, winning three of them (including on Polytrack; unraced on Tapeta); improved again to land 1m2f Listed race at Haydock and, while Diamond Rain has stronger form, this filly is certainly progressive and could yet scale loftier heights.
runners.comment: Progressive and lightly raced 4yo who was a Listed winner at Haydock last time
runners.owner: Hot To Trot Racing 2 & J Fill

runners.name: Sky Safari
runners.spotlight: Made it 6-7 on AW (including a victory on Tapeta) when winning the Group 3 Winter Derby at Lingfield (1m2f, Polytrack) in February; not at her best in Group 2 at Chester since but that was a hotter race than this and she can give a good account back in a Group 3 and back on the AW; 3lb penalty.
runners.comment: Impressive strike-rate on AW and won Group 3 Winter Derby at Lingfield in February

runners.name: Ashariba
runners.spotlight: Listed winner at Saint-Cloud (1m2f, soft) last October and reappeared with respectable Listed second in France (1m2f, soft) last month; has run well here in the past, but this 5yo's form needs to go to a new level today if she's to come out on top.
runners.comment: French Listed winner last October but this 5yo's form needs to go to a new level today

runners.name: Alla Stella
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who posted a Listed third when upped in trip at Nottingham (1m2f, good) on reappearance and soft ground may not have suited in Germany next time; she has something to find in today's company but is unexposed at 1m2f and 1-1 on AW (Polytrack); not ruled out each-way.
runners.comment: Listed third on reappearance; soft ground may not have suited last time; unexposed at 1m2f

runners.name: Uluru
runners.spotlight: Listed winner for Joseph O'Brien at Lingfield last June but not at the same level on final two outings for that yard and same story on first two starts for this stable.
runners.comment: Listed winner for Joseph O'Brien at Lingfield last June but hasn't shone since

races.race_name: JenningsBet In Bradford Gosforth Park Cup Handicap

runners.name: Corolla Point
runners.spotlight: Won 5f handicaps at Haydock (good) and Hamilton (soft) on his first two runs for Ed Bethell last September; beaten favourite in both starts this year, latterly at York six weeks ago which is a strong piece of form; no obvious reason why he won't handle AW and he is one to take seriously.
runners.comment: Unexposed and progressive sprinter; latest York third is rock-solid handicap form; player

runners.name: Fidelius
runners.spotlight: Six wins and five seconds from 17 AW runs; unplaced on turf on his last two starts and although this scenario will suit better, a few others look better handicapped.
runners.comment: Fine record on AW but looks high in the weights for a race of this nature

runners.name: Paddy's Day
runners.spotlight: Won this race 12 months ago off a 1lb higher mark and recorded his seventh C&D win when scoring in December; solid second on turf three weeks ago; should run his race.
runners.comment: Won this race off 1lb higher last year; solid turf run 3 weeks ago; should run his race

runners.name: Air Force One
runners.spotlight: Had some strong handicap form at York last summer; appreciated the return to turf when beating his nine rivals, including the odds-on Corolla Point, at Beverley (5f, good) in April; another solid York run when keeping on for fifth last month but slow starts are creeping in and he may prove vulnerable for win purposes back on AW.
runners.comment: Contender on turf best but needs to better earlier AW runs; slow starts becoming a theme

runners.name: Vantheman
runners.spotlight: Prominent racer; two wins this season, one of them C&D (2-2 here); back near his best when a close third of 17 at Thirsk last month but this is a stronger race.
runners.comment: 2-2 over C&D and arrives in good form; up in class though and a bigger effort is needed

runners.name: Gaeli
runners.spotlight: Ex-Italian 5yo; made a dream start for Marco Botti when powering home to win a 5f handicap at Southwell in March off just 2lb lower; that hasn't been a springboard to better things though and he could prove vulnerable; blinkers return after one run in cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Chance if reproducing form of March's Southwell win; hasn't hit that level 3 times since

runners.name: Kylian
runners.spotlight: Six AW runs have yielded four wins, a second and a close fifth (stable debut after a six-month break and off a 7lb higher mark than today's); has blown hot and cold on turf since April but the blinkers worn for his last four wins now return (has raced exclusively in cheekpieces since joining Robert Cowell) and he should make a bold bid to maintain an unbeaten C&D record.
runners.comment: Good record on AW, including 2-2 over C&D; dangerous mark; interesting contender
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing Xii & Mrs G Cowell

runners.name: Seven Questions
runners.spotlight: Capitalised on his drop in the weights when coming from off the pace to win at Goodwood (5f, good) last month; disappointed back at Goodwood (6f, soft) 19 days ago; stable has stronger claims elsewhere.
runners.comment: Talented but below best last time and his trainer looks to have stronger claims elsewhere

runners.name: Sir Yoshi
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish 4yo; best run for current stable when ninth at Thirsk last month; the return to 5f/AW won't faze him but this looks a warm race in which to end his losing streak.
runners.comment: Return to 5f/AW no problem but not sure he's in the form required to take this

runners.name: Purosangue
runners.spotlight: He hasn't won since a Listed success at York (6f, soft) in October 2023; in the meantime he has often attracted support, favourite in the 2024 Stewards' Cup and last season's Ayr Gold Cup; two perfectly creditable efforts for Robert Cowell this year and he showed promise in one Tapeta run last spring; not discounted for a trainer who runs four.
runners.comment: On a long losing run but two solid efforts for new yard and attractively weighted

runners.name: Canon's House
runners.spotlight: Made giant strides in 2025, winning four times including a big-field 5f Class 2 handicap at Southwell; only 1lb higher today but his 2026 campaign has been up and down and others are more appealing for win purposes.
runners.comment: Peak effort came on Tapeta last summer; in and out in 2026
runners.owner: M McHale, J Munroe, M Guy & D Walshe

runners.name: The Man
runners.spotlight: Looked a smart prospect last season, beating his 21 rivals at York's Dante meeting and second of 20 at the Ebor meeting; not at his best in three runs in Dubai but this mark should be in range and he has won on Tapeta and after a break; goes without accessories (usually hooded) today; interesting for a yard in form.
runners.comment: Not at his absolute best in Dubai this year but grounds to expect much better this time

runners.name: Al Shabab Storm
runners.spotlight: Smart on his day and his third at Wolverhampton (6f) last July saw him record an RPR of 115; down the field in two runs for Mick Appleby this year and he now drops to 5f for the first time in his career; tongue tied for the first time.
runners.comment: Smart on his day, including on Tapeta; has not shone for this yard and has first run at 5f

runners.name: Another Baar
runners.spotlight: On a tough mark after a big-race win in Bahrain in January; no impact in two runs this month and he is due to be 3lb lower from the weekend.
runners.comment: On a tough mark and he has finished down the field in two runs this month; plenty to prove

runners.name: Miss Rainbow
runners.spotlight: Course winner (6f); arrives having been in good form, seeing off her 11 rivals at Thirsk (5f, good) three weeks ago; a 4lb rise shouldn't prevent another solid effort.
runners.comment: Course winner who arrives having been in good form; prominent show seems likely

runners.name: Ancient State
runners.spotlight: Three wins and two seconds from five runs in 5f handicaps on AW; took his form to a new level when running away with a 6f handicap at Newmarket last week (eyeshields on for the first time); 4lb well in under a penalty; high on the list.
runners.comment: 5f on AW had seemed his optimum until he ran away with a 6f turf race last week; solid

runners.name: Little Mi Mi
runners.spotlight: Three wins in 2026, the first two over C&D; another career-best display when scoring at Ayr last month and her two defeats since were in Class 3 and 4 company; considered.
runners.comment: Two C&D wins this year; came up short in Class 3/4 on last two starts; this more suitable
runners.owner: N Irving, F Steele & I Jardine

runners.name: Call To Action
runners.spotlight: C&D winner; runner-up in two of his three starts this year; edging up the weights but he should give it a good go.
runners.comment: C&D winner; runner-up in two of his three starts this year; should give his running
runners.owner: D Scott, Resdev, Capla Dev & L Westwood

runners.name: Maldevious
runners.spotlight: 0-11 but she has performed with credit in each of her four 6f runs since undergoing wind surgery last November; close third off this mark at Hamilton last week; drops to 5f for the first time, with cheekpieces enlisted; each-way shout.
runners.comment: 0-11 but running well over 6f this year; drops in trip with headgear fitted; e-w shout

runners.name: Henery Hawk
runners.spotlight: Recorded his seventh AW win when landing a 5f handicap (0-60) at Wolverhampton in April; ran as well as he ever has on turf when third of six at Ayr 37 days ago; needs more in this company.
runners.comment: Multiple AW winner but usually in a lower grade; others are much stronger

runners.name: Tanjen
runners.spotlight: Two wins from three AW starts (including C&D) as opposed to 0-11 on turf; has a higher mark in this sphere as a result but it is probably still in range; one to note for market confidence.
runners.comment: Not at her best on turf this year but she's 2-3 on AW and could bounce back to form

runners.name: Harb
runners.spotlight: Two wins in 5f Class 5 handicaps in 2026, at this track in February and then when beating Fierce at Carlisle three weeks ago; stable form is a plus but he needs to bounce back from a quiet run at Hamilton.
runners.comment: C&D winner; below par at Hamilton latest but his trainer's form is an obvious plus

runners.name: Sports Coach
runners.spotlight: Now 4lb lower than when winning at Southwell (5f) in March; creditable efforts on turf more recently and he now drops into a Class 5; has a much better chance than his form figures might indicate.
runners.comment: Well handicapped and today's drop in class could spark a full revival

runners.name: Azuinthejungle
runners.spotlight: Reeled off a hat-trick last summer (5f-6f, turf and AW) and went close to landing a four-timer when second at Bath in August; below form this term (unable to live up to his market position on last two starts) and he is due to be 2lb lower from the weekend.
runners.comment: Thrived last summer; has not hit form in 2026, despite market prominence on last two runs
runners.owner: D P Van Der Hoeven & D G Pryde

runners.name: Fierce
runners.spotlight: Placed in all three runs this year, racing wide when third of nine at Beverley (5f, good) ten days ago; trainer, who also saddles Tiriac, has won the last two runnings of this race.
