runners.comment: Placed in all three starts this year; trainer seeking a hat-trick in this race; contender

runners.name: Tiriac
runners.spotlight: Can blow the start but he won two C&D handicaps in late 2025 and is well treated on that form; trainer has won the last two renewals of this race; high on the list.
runners.comment: Two C&D wins in late 2025; good mark; stable bidding for a hat-trick in this race

runners.name: Irish Nectar
runners.spotlight: His two standout efforts for this yard have come over 6f at Kempton; dropping into a Class 5 can help; each-way shout.
runners.comment: Drops into a Class 5 handicap for the first time; not discounted

runners.name: Blind Beggar
runners.spotlight: Tumbling down the weights and one to be interested in when the stable emerges from the doldrums.
runners.comment: On a good mark; one to be interested in when stable emerges from the doldrums
runners.owner: New Vision Bloodstock & Steel A Win

runners.name: Tickets
runners.spotlight: Unplaced elsewhere on final three starts for Ben Haslam but wasn't running badly and three out of his last four wins have come at this venue; on a career-low mark and could play a leading role back here in a first-time tongue-tie on his stable debut.
runners.comment: Likes it here and could play a leading role on his stable debut

runners.name: Jkr Cobbler
runners.spotlight: His last seven wins have come over 7f and the return to 6f isn't an obvious plus, but he's run well over 7f/1m at Carlisle on his two starts this month and has won here three times; might not be far away.
runners.comment: His last seven wins have come at 7f but he arrives in good form and might not be far away

runners.name: Angel Of England
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent 5yo whose losing run goes back 27 races and he was below par at Wetherby last week, however he was a close second at Ripon (6f, good) prior to that and is effective here; each-way claims if back on song.
runners.comment: Inconsistent and on a long losing run but went close two starts ago and is effective here
runners.owner: The Jocks And The Geordies II

runners.name: Tommy McJohn
runners.spotlight: Of interest on his good third at Dundalk (5f, Polytrack) in January on final Irish start; fairly encouraging fifth on stable debut at Ayr (5f, good to firm) last month; couldn't get into it at Southwell (6f, Tapeta) last time and he's just 1-17, but is not written off.
runners.comment: Couldn't get into it at Southwell last time but some encouraging runs previously

runners.name: Newyorkstateofmind
runners.spotlight: Course winner who was fourth of 13 in this last year and was a close second at Catterick last month; however, this inconsistent 9yo has been soundly beaten on his last two starts and has to leave those runs behind.
runners.comment: Course winner who was a close 2nd at Catterick last month but twice soundly beaten since

runners.name: Vitalline
runners.spotlight: Won a classified race at Kempton (7f, Polytrack) in March but he hasn't come up to scratch on his five starts since and needs to raise his game.
runners.comment: Won a classified race at Kempton in March but he hasn't come up to scratch since

runners.name: She's A Goldigger
runners.spotlight: Remains a maiden after 15 starts but she ran well when third in a first-time hood in a classified at Nottingham (6f, soft) a fortnight ago; unexposed over sprint trips and in the headgear, and she's not ruled out.
runners.comment: 0-15 but third at Nottingham recently and unexposed in the hood and over sprint trips

runners.name: Haworth Star
runners.spotlight: Just one win from his 20 starts but that came over C&D, and Tapeta brings out the best in him; not disgraced on last month's reappearance at Bath and he's down to a handy mark; of interest now back here.
runners.comment: Poor strike-rate but sole win came over C&D and he could build on his reappearance run

runners.name: Summer Rain
runners.spotlight: 0-12 and has failed to threaten on either start this year but when fifth of 14 at Beverley (7.4f, good) last Tuesday he was trapped wide and failed to settle; his best run last year came when 2l fourth at Wolverhampton (6f, Tapeta) in May off 8lb higher; the return to a sprint trip may bear fruit.
runners.comment: 0-12 and hasn't threatened this year but the return to a sprint trip may bear fruit

runners.name: Invincible Annice
runners.spotlight: Won off 4lb higher here (5f) last November; she's failed to get competitive subsequently but has a nice mark to work with if the first-time visor (replacing cheekpieces) prompts a revival; watch the betting.
runners.comment: Course winner; has a nice mark to work with if the first-time visor prompts a revival

runners.name: Ridgemaster
runners.spotlight: Returned to form when third at Ripon (6f, good) last month and again ran well when second at Ayr (7f, soft) last Saturday; his sole win came here and he could be involved.
runners.comment: Placed on turf on his last two starts and his sole win came here; could be involved

runners.name: Little Beck Annie
runners.spotlight: Displayed improved form in first-time cheekpieces to get off the mark at the eighth attempt at Carlisle (6f, good) last Monday; a 5lb penalty may not stop this 4yo if the headgear maintains its effect.
runners.comment: Won in first-time cheekpieces at Carlisle last week and a 5lb penalty may not stop her

runners.name: Miners Gamble
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far when fourth here (1m) in January and sixth here (1m2f) in March; mid-division at Doncaster (1m2f) later in March when last seen; drops back in trip after a break and is probably best watched.
runners.comment: Some good runs over 6f and 1m here this year but probably best watched back down to 6f

runners.name: Sally Anne's Dream
runners.spotlight: 0-12 and hasn't achieved much; this 5yo needs a career-best if she's to win today on her stable and seasonal debut.
runners.comment: 0-12 and hasn't achieved much; this 5yo needs a career-best on her stable/seasonal debut

runners.name: Trean
runners.spotlight: Bayside Boy colt made rapid late gains for a 1l third of 17 in a Fairyhouse maiden on debut two weeks ago; should be more streetwise with that under his belt and sets the standard on form.
runners.comment: Promising third on debut at Fairyhouse, wiser now, sets the standard on form

runners.name: Desert Alchemist
runners.spotlight: Promise on debut at Cork (6f, soft) but didn't build on that when soundly beaten at Naas dropped to 5f on quicker ground; others make more appeal now.
runners.comment: Didn't build on promising debut when soundly beaten at Naas, others appeal more

runners.name: Abraham Lincoln
runners.spotlight: Wootton Bassett colt; E2,300,000 yearling; half-brother to winners Highbury (Group-placed 1m4f; RPR 111), Friendly Face (French/Swiss Listed-placed 1m1f-10.5f; 100) and Mille Fois Merci (French 1m4f; 78); dam French 5.5f (Group 3) and 7f 2yo winner (111); one of three for top yard, Group 1 entered and the choice of Moore.
runners.comment: E2,300,000 Wotton Bassett colt, the choice of Moore, holds Group 1 entry

runners.name: Walk On Harry
runners.spotlight: Earthlight colt; E32,000 yearling; half-brother to winners Letsbefrankaboutit (6f 2yo Group 3; RPR 103), Lady Ayresome (5f 2yo; 86), Moojim (5f-6f inc 2yo/Sweden; 86) and Miyagi (KSA 7f/10.5f); dam 6f 2yo winner (70); showed ability when fifth of 10 in Naas barrier trial; not ruled out.
runners.comment: E32,000 Earthlight colt, dam 2yo winner, ability when fifth of 10 in Naas barrier trial

runners.name: Anchor Road
runners.spotlight: Dark Angel colt; E120,000 foal, E280,000 yearling; closely related to 5.7f-7f winner Beauty Crescent (inc 2yo Listed/HK; RPR 105), half-brother to four winners inc Worship (6f AW 2yo; 100); dam 6f-1m winner (inc 2yo/US 1m Grade 2; 108); likely type on paper for yard that has won four of the last five renewals.
runners.comment: Dark Angel colt, E280,000 yearling, dam 6f-1m 2yo winner, likely type on paper

runners.name: Hi Corbett Court
runners.spotlight: Starman colt is one of two for yard; ran green when a 9.25l third of 9 on debut at Navan (6f, good) when an 11-2 shot; tongue tie goes on; looks the stable's second string on rider arrangements.
runners.comment: Modest third on debut at Navan, tongue tie on, looks second string on rider arrangements

runners.name: Revelator
runners.spotlight: Starman colt; E12,000 yearling; half-brother to Listed-placed 6f 2yo winner Figjam (RPR 91); dam 1m/8.6f AW winner (79), half-sister to useful 6f-1m5f winner Karam Albaari; displayed promise when third in Naas barrier trial.
runners.comment: E12,000 Starman colt, dam 1m/8.6f winner, promise when third in Naas barrier trial

runners.name: Haffner
runners.spotlight: Justify colt; 275,000USD yearling; half-brother to US 7.5f turf 2yo winner Orbit; dam 7f 2yo winner (RPR 91), half-sister to winners Order Of St George (Gold Cup and Irish St Leger), Asperity (1m Group 3) and Angel Terrace (US 8.5f Grade 3); one of three newcomers for top yard; overlooked by Moore.
runners.comment: 275,000USD Justify colt, dam 7f 2yo winner, one of three for yard, overlooked by Moore

runners.name: Jewel In A Crowd
runners.spotlight: Hello Youmzain colt sent off at 7-2 on debut at Cork (5f, soft) but was beaten 8l into fifth of 10; top rider booked and steps up in trip now on faster ground but likely best watched.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten when 7-2 on debut at Cork (5f, soft), faster ground now and upped in trip
runners.owner: Exors of the Late Mrs Dolores Gleeson

runners.name: Man's Best Friend
runners.spotlight: No Nay Never colt; brother to 6f/7f winner Hamlet (inc AW; RPR 86), closely related to 7f AW 2yo winner Velazquez (85), half-brother to 1m-1m4f winner Perfect Your Craft (inc 2yo; 97); dam Listed-placed 1m2f winner (103); overlooked by Moore but Group 1 entered and not discounted.
runners.comment: No Nay Never colt, dam listed placed 1m2f winner, Gr' 1 entered, Moore prefers stablemate
runners.owner: M Tabor & D Smith & Mrs J Magnier & Westerberg

runners.name: Prince Of Peppard
runners.spotlight: Starman colt; E50,000 yearling; half-brother to winners See All Match (Qatari 8.5f/1m2f) and Petarda (Italian/Slovakian 8.5f/1m1f Flat inc Listed; 2m-2m3f hurdle); dam unplaced 7f-1m4f (RPR 65); probably best watched unless market speaks.
runners.comment: E50,000 Starman colt, dam unplaced 7f-1m4f, watch unless the market speaks

runners.name: Tina Tereshkova
runners.spotlight: Huge odds and well beaten in all three previous starts; seems safe to rule out.
runners.comment: Huge odds and well beaten in three previous starts, seems safe to rule out

races.race_name: Diageo Luxury Spirits Irish EBF Median Auction Maiden

runners.name: Bang Bang
runners.spotlight: Solid in two runs as a 2yo; unlucky-in-running on return at Leopardstown but ran a fine race there last time over 1m2f; caught close home then after leading in the straight so the drop to 1m should suit; a player.
runners.comment: Caught close home over 1m2f last time so the drop to 1m should suit; a player

runners.name: Karrygrant
runners.spotlight: Has run some solid races in defeat up to 1m2f; faced a tough task at Gowran last time but ran creditably; the hood worn then is left off and the drop back to 1m should be fine so is a player.
runners.comment: Has run some solid races in defeat up to 1m2f; drop back to 1m should be fine so a player

runners.name: Loughrea
runners.spotlight: Made a promising debut at Leopardstown last autumn and built on that on return when a close second in a Gowran maiden over this trip; a leading player with improvement to come.
runners.comment: Close second in a Gowran maiden over this trip; a leading player with improvement to come

runners.name: Valentino Eclipse
runners.spotlight: Was strong in the market on debut when just denied over 7f at Leopardstown; the form of that race has taken some knocks but he should have improved since and the step up in trip should be fine.
runners.comment: The form of debut has had some knocks but he should have improved since; should stay trip

runners.name: Rock Of Claremont
runners.spotlight: Closely related to 7f 2yo winner Royal Meeting (inc Group 1; RPR 116), half-brother to four winners inc Heavy Metal (5f-1m inc 2yo turf Group 2/UAE dirt Group 2; 119); dam SA 6f Grade 1 winner/UAE 1,000 Guineas third (95); 30,000gns yearling has a quality international damline; looks yard's first-string so needs a market check.
runners.comment: Has a quality international damline; looks yard's first-string so needs a market check

runners.name: King Of Eze
runners.spotlight: Second foal; dam 9.4f/1m2f winner (inc 2yo/AW; RPR 87), half-sister to 1m Listed winner Staying On, from the family of Oaks winner Talent; 3,000gns foal was a 1,000gns vendor buyback as a yearling; looks the stable second-string on debut.
runners.comment: A 1,000gns vendor buyback as a yearling; looks the stable second-string on debut

runners.name: Lord Darragh
runners.spotlight: Third foal; dam unplaced 1m 2yo (RPR 61), half-sister to Listed-placed 9.5f 2yo winner Delhi, out of Group-placed 6f 2yo winner; not a bad pedigree and cost E10,000 as a yearling; yard sprang a 25/1 shock in this race last year so not ruled out.
runners.comment: A E10,000 yearling; yard sprang a 25/1 shock in this race last year so not ruled out

runners.name: Save The Sixes
runners.spotlight: Eighth foal; half-sister to five winners inc Anda Muchacho (Italian 1m-1m2f inc Group 2; RPR 112), Parsley (5f/6f 2yo; 99) and Love Powerful (6f inc 2yo; 91); decent pedigree and cost 220,000gns as a yearling; worth a market check for an in-form yard.
runners.comment: Decent pedigree and cost 220,000gns as a yearling; market check for an in-form yard

runners.name: Gliondar
runners.spotlight: Sixth foal; half-brother to French winners Metropolitan (1m inc 2yo/French 2,000 Guineas; RPR 118) and Mister Charlie (7.5f/1m inc AW; 81); dam unraced sister to 1m3f winner Dorfman; cost E150,000 as a yearling; makes appeal on paper so needs a check in the market for a yard flying high.
runners.comment: Half-brother to French 2,000 Guineas winner Metropolitan; needs a check in the market

runners.name: Ebony Reign
runners.spotlight: Beaten 17l on debut over 7f at Leopardstown recently and will have to step up plenty on that.
runners.comment: Beaten 17l on debut over 7f at Leopardstown recently and will have to step up plenty

runners.name: Noble Venture
runners.spotlight: Was beaten 20l on debut over 1m2f at Fairyhouse so would have to make huge progress in three weeks.
runners.comment: Beaten 20l on debut over 1m2f at Fairyhouse so would have to make huge progress in 3 weeks

runners.name: Good Man Jack
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in two maidens and will be of more interest in handicaps after this.
runners.comment: Well beaten in two maidens and will be of more interest in handicaps

runners.name: Wheres The Gold
runners.spotlight: Poor in two maidens in 2024 and not a lot better on return at Limerick a week ago; wait for handicaps.
runners.comment: Poor in two maidens in 2024 and not a lot better on return a week ago; wait for handicaps

runners.name: Chapel Lane
runners.spotlight: Consistent in defeat in three maidens last year; beaten 12l on return in a 1m2f Down Royal handicap; visor returns down in trip and needs his best.
runners.comment: Beaten 12l on return in a 1m2f Down Royal handicap; visor returns down in trip; needs best

runners.name: Highbury See See
runners.spotlight: Won handicaps at Gowran and over C&D last year, both with plenty of cut in the ground; some fine efforts in defeat this term including on good ground; needs a bit more but considered.
runners.comment: C&D winner on soft; some fine efforts in defeat this term; needs a bit more but considered

runners.name: Neo Smart
runners.spotlight: Dual AW winner gained her sole turf win from 27 starts over 7f at this venue last year; soundly beaten on last three starts and needs a return to best.
runners.comment: Course winner over 7f; soundly beaten on last three starts and needs a return to best

runners.name: Calm The Jets
runners.spotlight: Won a Dundalk maiden on debut in January 2025 over this trip; then went missing for over a year and has to do a lot better than last two efforts.
runners.comment: Missing for over a year after debut AW win; has to do a lot better than last two efforts

runners.name: Majestic King
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark in a Gowran handicap over this trip and was a close second at Ballinrobe (1m1.5f) under a 7lb penalty after; was poor at Leopardstown since so questions now.
runners.comment: Won at Gowran and a close second at Ballinrobe; questions after a poor Leopardstown run

runners.name: Liberation Date
runners.spotlight: Won a 7f Dundalk maiden on third start last December; was poor at the same track next time; came back after a break with a fine effort over C&D when a bit keen; needs a bit more but a chance off 2lb lower.
runners.comment: Fine effort over C&D when a bit keen; needs a bit more but a chance off 2lb lower

runners.name: Therewillbeglory
runners.spotlight: Has been consistent in defeat in six maidens up to 1m2.5f; carried her head awkwardly at Dundalk last time and a visor is applied; needs her best on handicap bow.
runners.comment: Carried head awkwardly at Dundalk last time and a visor is applied; needs her best

runners.name: No More Porter
runners.spotlight: All three wins have been here including one over C&D; was third on return over C&D but was poor at Leopardstown since so has questions to answer now.
runners.comment: Third on return over C&D but was poor at Leopardstown since so has questions to answer now

runners.name: Spanish Rising
runners.spotlight: Was runner-up in a Ripon maiden over 1m2f last August on second start for former yard; well below that level in two runs for this yard but faster ground back to 1m may suit.
runners.comment: Well below his best in two runs for this yard but faster ground back to 1m may suit

runners.name: Nautic Star
runners.spotlight: Made a promising debut over 7f here last year behind the brilliant Diamond Necklace; didn't seem to handle soft ground next time at Cork; well beaten by a smart rival in a Dundalk maiden on return; considered on handicap debut and the market could help.
runners.comment: Some decent maiden form; considered on handicap debut and the market could help
runners.owner: J Kirkland & Mrs J M Ryan

runners.name: Banner Beauty
runners.spotlight: Won a Leopardstown maiden over this trip last year; was below her best on final two starts of 2025; has to improve on her C&D comeback run last time.
runners.comment: Below her best on final two starts of 2025; has to improve on her C&D comeback run
runners.owner: O B P Carroll & Anthony Vaughan

runners.name: Blues Emperor
runners.spotlight: Five wins between turf and AW for Johnny Murtagh; best effort for this yard came when fourth over 7f here but has been below that level twice again since; blinkers added.
runners.comment: Fourth over 7f here but has been below that level twice again since; blinkers added

runners.name: Chester Nimitz
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark for former yard in a 6f Navan maiden last October; has placed form at this trip but needs to do better than on his two starts for this yard.
runners.comment: Sole win over 6f; needs to do better than on his two starts for this yard

runners.name: Prevalence
runners.spotlight: Won a Dundalk maiden over 7f on second start last winter; a fine effort on handicap/seasonal debut over 7f here but wasn't as good over C&D since when raced keenly; questions now.
runners.comment: Fine effort over 7f here but wasn't as good over C&D since when raced keenly; questions

runners.name: Usuario Amigo
runners.spotlight: Sole win came in a 7f Brighton maiden in 2024; some fair runs for this yard since last year but has to improve on recent form.
runners.comment: Some fair runs for this yard since last year but has to improve on recent form

runners.name: Tachos
runners.spotlight: Dual winner over 1m1f but has placed form at this trip too; way below her best in two starts this year but this faster ground will suit and can give an improved effort.
runners.comment: Way below her best in two starts this year but this faster ground will suit so do better

runners.name: Tofino
runners.spotlight: Sole win came over C&D last year off 4lb lower; several decent efforts in defeat since including at Limerick last time though looked a bit awkward under pressure then; still, unlikely to be far away.
runners.comment: C&D winner; looked a bit awkward under pressure last time; still, unlikely to be far away

runners.name: Brewing
runners.spotlight: All six wins have been gained on AW when trained in UK; some fine runs in defeat for this yard on AW over the winter; soft ground was against him the last twice and can do better.
runners.comment: Six AW wins; soft ground was against him the last twice and can do better

runners.name: Albion Power
runners.spotlight: Dual AW winner has only had three starts on turf but didn't run badly over C&D last time; still needs a fair bit more dropped 2lb.
runners.comment: Didn't run badly over C&D last time; still needs a fair bit more dropped 2lb

runners.name: Liberate
runners.spotlight: Won a valuable 1m2f Goodwood novice for former yard in 2025 but lost her form latterly; the best of four runs for this yard came over C&D last time and can do better so considered.
runners.comment: Best of four runs for this yard came over C&D last time and can do better so considered

runners.name: Summer Snow
runners.spotlight: Won three times last year up to a mile; hasn't been near that level this year but last time at Down Royal was a bit better; needs a return to best.
runners.comment: Hasn't been near best this year but last time at Down Royal was a bit better

runners.name: Thyer
runners.spotlight: Two wins over this trip when trained in UK; soundly beaten in four runs over various trips for this yard last year; needs his best on return but the lively ground will suit.
runners.comment: Disappointing late last year; needs his best on return but the lively ground will suit

runners.name: Smoke Them Out
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner at 6f; made all to win on testing ground here in March; solid effort on latest at Cork (7f, sft-hvy) when a 1.5l second of 8; dropped 1lb for that but would prefer softer ground than this.
runners.comment: Made all over 6f here in March on heavy, good 7f run at Cork latest, prefers softer ground

runners.name: Thru And Thru
runners.spotlight: 0-7 on turf but has races in her; placed on handicap debut and seasonal return at Naas off 5lb higher (7f, soft); below best twice since, however, and faster ground a concern here (unraced on quicker than yielding).
runners.comment: 0-7 but some fine runs in defeat, below best last twice, unraced on quicker than yielding

runners.name: Summer Island
runners.spotlight: In fine form this year; won over C&D (good) early last month off 9lb lower; solid effort in defeat over 1m (good) off revised mark here on latest; drop back to 7f no problem and top rider booked; should be thereabouts again.
runners.comment: C&D win off 9lb lower last month, fine run off revised mark latest, thereabouts again
runners.owner: Nigel O'Hare & Martin W White & Mark Devlin & C G Bryson

runners.name: Spacebound
runners.spotlight: Handicap debutant; promise in all three maidens at 7f-1m on AW and on easy ground on turf; had Zenford 5l behind when runner up over C&D in March and he has since advertised that form; respected, especially if strong in the betting.
runners.comment: H'cap debutant, promise in all three maidens, form of C&D second boosted, respected

runners.name: Invincible Will
runners.spotlight: Gained second career win on latest over C&D (good) and the runner up Zenford has won since; 5lb rise for that short-head success seems fair and his C&D form figures now read 311; should be in the mix again.
runners.comment: C&D win latest, form boosted, up 5lb, C&D form figures read 311, in the mix again

runners.name: Collecting Coin
runners.spotlight: Galway maiden winner last July (7f, yielding); promising start in handicaps on easy ground this term but progress stalled on quicker ground at Cork on latest in first-time cheekpieces; may prefer softer ground.
runners.comment: Galway maiden winner, best handicap efforts on easy ground, might prefer it softer

runners.name: Little Empire
runners.spotlight: 2-6 on turf on the Flat, both wins coming on quick ground at 7f-1m at Ayr; remains 10lb above his last turf win but not beaten far off 2lb higher on latest at Musselburgh (7f, good); conditions to suit here and he could go well.
runners.comment: Two-time winner on fast ground at Ayr, decent run at Musselburgh latest, could go well

runners.name: Apache Outlaw
runners.spotlight: Dual turf winner has shaped nicely enough in two runs since return from a two-month break; not beaten that far when fourth of 15 at Leopardstown latest (7f, good) and place claims if backing that up.
runners.comment: Not beaten far at Leopardstown on latest, place claims if backing that up

runners.name: Alfred Tennyson
runners.spotlight: Good ground 7f maiden winner for former yard; narrowly denied over 6f (good) at Navan on latest; return to 7f no problem but 2lb higher now and these are deeper waters; more needed to get head back in front.
runners.comment: Narrowly denied off 2lb lower at Navan latest (6f), return to 7f a plus but this harder

runners.name: Tolebi
runners.spotlight: Smart form in France for Andre Fabre; best effort for this yard on latest, a 4l fourth of 6 in conditions race at Fairyhouse when racing keenly; rider claims a handy 5lb and could go well back in handicap company.
runners.comment: Best run yet for this yard on latest, rider takes off  handy 5lb, could go well

runners.name: Indigo Dream
runners.spotlight: 7f AW maiden winner; 0-9 on turf but has finished runner up five times, including over 6f at Naas (good) in April; same mark now and didn't enjoy the smoothest of passages in two runs here since; could go well with Keane up.
runners.comment: Runner up off this mark at Naas in April, traffic problems here last twice, could go well

runners.name: Una Matata
runners.spotlight: Four-time AW winner is 1-13 on turf; pleasing effort when a 2l fifth of 14 at Down Royal (7f, good) in May when not getting the clearest of runs and same mark now; this a tougher race, though, so more needed.
runners.comment: 1-13 on turf, good run at Down Royal in May, same mark but this is much tougher
runners.owner: Mark Devlin & Mark McGuckin & Claire Wylie

runners.name: Spodo Komodo
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the fourth attempt in maidens at this venue when last sighted in September (6f, yld-sft); has placed form at 7f on good ground so worth checking in the market on handicap debut and seasonal reappearance.
runners.comment: 6f maiden winner here last Sept', off since, 7f/good ground fine, check market

runners.name: Arryaaf
runners.spotlight: Won on the dirt over 7f at Jebel Ali in March 2025 for former yard; unraced on turf; off since November and not easy to assess on stable and handicap debut; market can guide.
runners.comment: 7f dirt winner, off since November, turf/stable/handicap debut, market best guide

runners.name: Gypsy Queen
runners.spotlight: Fairyhouse maiden winner in May 2025 (6f, good); failed to fire on handicap bow at Gowran when last seen in June 2025 (7f, soft) but ground may not have suited; worth a market check on first start since.
runners.comment: Good ground maiden winner, off since below par run on soft last June, check market

runners.name: Um Wadi
runners.spotlight: AW winner in France (8.5f); ran well at Dundalk in March (7f) but no impact on turf the last twice, albeit was out of depth in Listed race at Gowran last time; tongue tie now tried.
runners.comment: AW winner in France, out of depth in Listed heat at Gowran latest, tongue tie now tried
runners.owner: Mark Feighery & Kevin Blake & Paul McKeon

runners.name: Exquisite Acclaim
runners.spotlight: Eight-time AW winner; not as good on turf (1-31) and last of 12 on his favoured surface when last seen at Lingfield in early April; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Eight AW wins, 1-31 on turf, last of 12 at Lingfield on latest on April, others preferred
runners.owner: Thomas J O'Connor & Ms Hazel McGuinness

runners.name: Dar Toungi
runners.spotlight: Eight wins in France included a Listed race in April 2024; poor form over hurdles and on the Flat since joining this yard; hard to fancy but mark sliding and worth monitoring in the market.
runners.comment: Listed winner in France, poor so far over hurdles and Flat for this yard, check the market

runners.name: Faro Island
runners.spotlight: Two-time winner on easy ground, including on latest at Gowran (9.5f, yielding); won over 1m3f (yielding) at Down Royal last season; 3lb rise for latest success seems reasonable and worth a try at this trip but could probably do with some rain.
runners.comment: Two-time winner on yielding, 3lb rise for Gowran win on latest, rain would boost claims

runners.name: Tatum
runners.spotlight: Two handicap wins last season, both at Leopardstown over 1m4f (good) and 1m5f (good); remains 4lb above the latest of those wins but 2m1f may have stretched last time at Limerick and he drops back in trip now; player.
runners.comment: Two wins at Leopardstown last year (1m4f/1m5f), 2m1f too far latest, drops in trip, player

runners.name: Cooley's Mist
runners.spotlight: 0-7 but has a race in him based on his two handicap runs at Leopardstown, including on latest when beaten 5.5l into third of 15 (1m1f, soft, Pillar Of Hope fourth); unproven at this trip, chance if he stays (not certain on breeding).
runners.comment: 0-7, promising run over 1m1f on latest, stamina to prove for this trip, chance if stays

runners.name: Eastern Peace
runners.spotlight: AW winner; promising effort on return from break at Leopardstown on latest (1m2f, good), rallying for a 2l third of 11; well worth a go at this trip on that evidence and 1lb lower now too.
runners.comment: AW winner, rallied for third over 1m2f on seasonal return, 1lb lower now, 1m4f could suit
runners.owner: Martin W Walsh & Tony Carey & Brian Murray & Mark Hopkins

runners.name: Keepsgettingbetter
runners.spotlight: 3yo filly; progressive since sent handicapping; notched up wins at Dundalk over 8f and 10.5f before fine effort off 2lb lower at Leopardstown on latest (1m4f, good); could be more to come with tongue tie applied.
runners.comment: 3yo progressive in h'caps, fine effort off 2lb lower latest, tongue tie on, more to come
runners.owner: Mrs Richard Henry & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Tassarolo
runners.spotlight: First reserve; dual-purpose; 1-10 on turf on the Flat; okay efforts on last three starts on the level; upset in stalls when 6l eighth of 17 at Fairyhouse on latest; handicapper slow to relent.
runners.comment: 1-10 on turf, respectable runs on last three starts, needs help from handicapper; reserve

runners.name: Celtic Druid
runners.spotlight: Two-time AW winner (both 1m4f) is 1-16 on turf; good effort behind Kool One on penultimate at Navan (1m5f, gd-yld); didn't get the smoothest of runs when midfield at Gowran latest; drop back to 1m4f a plus and not out of this.
runners.comment: Two-time 1m4f AW winner, 1-16 on turf, troubled passage on latest, not out of this

runners.name: Derry Lad
runners.spotlight: Ground versatile sort is on losing run since 2023 and recent starts have not suggested a return to winning ways is imminent; is at least well handicapped but revival needed; declared at Naas 7.10 Wednesday.
runners.comment: On losing run since 2023, well handicapped but needs to rediscover best form

runners.name: Kool One
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose; 3-14 on the Flat; won off 3lb lower on penultimate at Navan (1m5f, gd-yld); decent effort off revised mark over 1m6f here on latest (good), staying on for a 6.75l seventh of 24; won over this distance last August so drop in trip not a concern; in the mix.
runners.comment: 1m4f winner, scored over 1m5f at Navan on penultimate, decent run over 1m6f here latest

runners.name: Covert Legend
runners.spotlight: AW winner in Britain (8.5f) is 0-4 on turf and yet to hit the frame; new yard now (bought for 5,500gns) and likely best watched on first run for 239 days unless the market speaks.
runners.comment: British AW winner, 0-4 on turf, off 239 days, new yard, watch unless market speaks

runners.name: Granville Street
runners.spotlight: Hurdles winner is 1-11 on turf on the Flat; on long losing run but went close behind Kool One at Navan in April (1m5f, gd-yld); respectable efforts twice since but more needed to get head back in front; cheekpieces tried.
runners.comment: 1-11 on turf, went close behind Kool One at Navan, ok runs twice since, needs more

runners.name: Nod Of Approval
runners.spotlight: 3yo was narrowly denied by Darius Dark at Cork (1m4f, good) in April and is weighted to reverse that form; weakened when upped to 2m1f at Limerick (yielding) on latest; drop in trip and return to quicker ground both positives; big player.
runners.comment: 3yo, close second to Darius Dark on penultimate, trip and ground excuses latest, chance

runners.name: Quatre Bras
runners.spotlight: Three-time AW winner is yet to score on turf (0-20) but has run well in defeat at times; best form on the grass has come on easy ground but last three runs have not been encouraging; others arrive in better form.
runners.comment: Three-time AW winner, 0-20 on turf, best form on easy ground, below best on recent starts

runners.name: Gaelic Drills
runners.spotlight: 3yo; handicap debutant; glimmers of ability in trio of maidens on varying ground at around 1m2f; worth a market check upped in trip.
runners.comment: 3yo h'cap debutant, glimmers of ability in 1m2f maidens, worth market check upped in trip

runners.name: Cracking Cloud
runners.spotlight: Handicap debutant; best effort in trio of 9.5f maidens came on soft ground at Gowran on debut; dam 1m4f winner so could improve for this longer trip; check the market.
runners.comment: H'cap debut, best maiden run came on debut on soft, dam won over this trip, check market

runners.name: Darius Dark
runners.spotlight: 3yo gelding is 2-2 in handicaps against his own age group; won over 1m4f at Cork (good) and followed up off 5lb higher at Leopardstown (1m4f, good); raised another 6lb and takes on older rivals now but could be more to come in first-time cheekpieces; leading claims.
runners.comment: 2/2 in 3yo handicaps, up 6lb for Leop' win latest, may be more to come; cheekpieces on

runners.name: Sea Eagle
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose; two-time Flat winner in 2024 off 2lb lower at Roscommon over 10.5f (good) and off 3lb lower 7.5f (heavy); in process of running a big race on return from break when unseating in maiden hurdle at Tramore in April; could go well.
runners.comment: Two-time Flat winner in 2024, running big when unseating over hurdles latest, may go well

runners.name: Party Dress
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose; 2-15 on the Flat, the latest win coming over 2m1f (good) at Limerick last June off 2lb lower; pulled up in chase after break on latest but ran respectably in this last year off 6lb higher so could run into the money.
runners.comment: 2-15 on Flat, ran respectably in this last year off 6lb higher, could run into the money

runners.name: Avoca Woods
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; very ordinary form in maidens at 1m-1m4f on variety of surfaces; can only be watched on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Ordinary form in maidens at 1m-1m4f, can only be watched in first handicap; reserve

runners.name: Royal Alliance
runners.spotlight: Dropped into 0-60 company and back to form when a 0.5l third of 14 at Down Royal (1m5f, soft) a week ago; needs to back that up now on quicker ground in this tougher grade.
runners.comment: Revival in 0-60 on latest on soft ground, quicker ground now and this much tougher

runners.name: Pillar Of Hope
runners.spotlight: 5-25 on turf including this race last year off 10lb lower under this rider; shaped like coming back to form when meeting traffic problems when fourth of 15 at Leopardstown on latest; eased 2lb for that and place shout at least.
runners.comment: Won this last year off 10lb lower, shaped like coming back to form latest, chance

runners.name: Folly Beach
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; dual-purpose; 1-25 on turf on the Flat; back to form with neck second of 14 at Down Royal (1m3f, soft) six days ago; her win came on soft so quicker conditions a concern for her here.
runners.comment: Soft ground winner, back to form on that ground on latest, quicker conditions now; reserve

runners.name: Devine Genius
runners.spotlight: Maiden; standout effort for former yard a 3l third of 9 in 7f Tipperary maiden (soft) in May 2024; no show in two starts last year and bought by current connections for E3,000 in November; lacks a run and can only be watched.
runners.comment: Maiden, ran poorly in two starts last year, new yard now, lacks a run, best watched

races.race_name: McGuinness Maintenance Services Irish EBF Maiden

runners.name: Changing Lanes
runners.spotlight: The best of three maiden runs was when just denied at Roscommon over an extended 1m2f; keen and might not have stayed 1m5f at Leopardstown since but still a fine effort; tongue-tie added and is a leading player back down in trip.
runners.comment: Best run when just denied at Roscommon over an extended 1m2f; back to a similar trip now

runners.name: Loch Gamhna
runners.spotlight: Was only 2.25l behind the classy Causeway in a soft ground maiden over 7f on second start last year; hasn't really built on that though some respectable runs since; rated 82 so shouldn't be far away.
runners.comment: Hasn't really built on early promise but some respectable runs; shouldn't be far away

runners.name: Wyman
runners.spotlight: Some promise in two runs last year; improved effort on return over this trip at Leopardstown; the tongue-tie worn then is retained and shouldn't be far away with improvement.
runners.comment: Improved effort on return over this trip at Leopardstown and shouldn't be far away

runners.name: Hard Promises
runners.spotlight: A fine effort on debut at Cork over this trip on soft ground; there should be improvement to come from that and can be a player if as effective on this faster surface; Colin Keane aboard and one of three here for the yard.
runners.comment: A fine effort on debut at Cork over this trip; improvement to come and can be a player

runners.name: Numantia
runners.spotlight: Was an eyecatcher on debut over C&D last month, finishing just 3/4l behind the re-opposing Saxony Charms; the same terms today so there shouldn't be much between them again and likely to be involved.
runners.comment: Was an eyecatcher on debut over C&D last month and likely to be involved

runners.name: Saxony Charms
runners.spotlight: Third runner for yard; well-bred sort ran a fine race on debut over C&D last month, 3/4l in front of the re-opposing Numantia; should have improved since and likely to be a major player.
runners.comment: Ran a fine race on debut over C&D last month; should have improved since so a major player
runners.owner: Team Valor International LLC & A Oliver

runners.name: Will You Stop
runners.spotlight: Both starts at Gowran; improved nicely from his debut over 1m when third over 1m1.5f next time; there should be further improvement and can be involved if staying this longer trip.
runners.comment: Third over 1m1.5f at Gowran last time; there should be further improvement; be involved

runners.name: Tokyo Tower
runners.spotlight: Second foal; dam German Listed-placed 1m3f winner, half-sister to German 1m2f Group 3 winner Sea Of Sands, out of 1m2f-10.7f winning sister to US 8.5f Grade 3 winner Salve Germania; cost E41,000 as a foal; respected connections and a notable jockey booking so market strength should be noted.
runners.comment: Respected connections and a notable jockey booking so market strength should be noted

runners.name: Youcansaythatagain
runners.spotlight: Eleventh foal; half-brother to six winners inc Caponata (9.4f-1m2f inc Listed; RPR 113), Brooch (1m-9.4f inc Group 2; 110) and Raymonda (7f inc 2yo; 107); dam French 5f Listed/5.5f 2yo winner (101); well-bred sort cost 32,000gns as a yearling; interesting to see his market position compared to two stablemates.
runners.comment: Well-bred sort; interesting to see his market position compared to two stablemates

runners.name: Collared
runners.spotlight: Second foal; dam once-raced sister to Champion Stakes winner Fascinating Rock, closely related to 5f/6f winner Marwari, half-sister to smart 1m4f-2m Flat and 2m hurdle winner Quick Jack; is by a leading sire and from a quality damline; worth checking for market strength on debut.
runners.comment: By a leading sire and from a quality damline; worth checking for market strength on debut

runners.name: Zadawa
runners.spotlight: Made a pleasing debut when fourth (beaten nearly 7l) over 1m1.5f at Gowran two weeks ago; can improve from that but others still hold stronger form claims.
runners.comment: Can improve from Gowran debut run but others still hold stronger form claims
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Scat Rhythm
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; half-brother to winners incl' Special Wan (6.5f-1m inc US turf Grade 3; RPR 118), Yerwanthere (7f/1m inc AW/US; 108) and Over The Blues (Listed-placed 6f; 96); related to some smart types; late starter but worth a market check.
runners.comment: Related to some smart types; late starter but worth a market check

runners.name: Solas Na Gealai
runners.spotlight: Promise on debut over 1m4f at this course but might not have handled soft ground when was poor at Down Royal since; others have stronger credentials.
runners.comment: Might not have handled soft ground when was poor at Down Royal last time; others preferred

runners.name: Pimlico Piranha
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in two maidens and is a more likely candidate for handicaps.
runners.comment: Well beaten in two maidens and is a more likely candidate for handicaps

runners.name: Ballysax Stew
runners.spotlight: Pulled-up in a maiden hurdle in 2024 and well beaten in a Flat maiden on return; cheekpieces on; best watched.
runners.comment: Pulled-up in a maiden hurdle in 2024; well beaten in a Flat maiden on return; just watch

races.race_name: O'Driscolls Irish Whiskey Irish EBF Stayers Handicap

runners.name: Too Bossy For Us
runners.spotlight: One of two for yard that has won two of the last four renewals; useful hurdler was well held on return to the Flat behind Poetic Sound here on latest (1m6f, yielding) but was runner up in this last year on good ground off 1lb higher; dangerous to discount.
runners.comment: Useful hurdler, runner up in this last year off 1lb higher, dangerous to discount

runners.name: Delta
runners.spotlight: 1m4f Fairyhouse maiden winner last season (gd-yld); promising seasonal reappearance here 33 days ago, staying on for a 5.5l fourth of 24 over 1m6f (2l behind Kilmeaden in third); well worth a go at this trip on that evidence and mark unchanged; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: 1m4f maiden winner, stayed on over 1m6f here latest, 2m could suit, cheekpieces added

runners.name: Robbies Rock
runners.spotlight: Comfortable 3l win over 1m6f (good) at this venue off 5lb higher last May but wasn't seen again in 2025; soundly beaten in two runs this term at 1m4f/1m6f and needs to revive now upped in trip.
runners.comment: Won off 5lb higher over 1m6f here last May, soundly beaten two runs in 2026, must revive

runners.name: Kilmeaden
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose; hurdle winner is 1-9 on the Flat; has come up dry since winning a Galway Festival maiden last July (12.5f, yielding); kept on nicely for third over 1m6f here latest (Delta behind); raised 2lb for that but should go well again up in trip.
runners.comment: Kept on nicely over 1m6f here latest, raised 2lb, moves up in trip, go well again

runners.name: Ballyadam
runners.spotlight: Veteran; talented hurdler a latecomer to the Flat but showed promise in maidens and bolted up on handicap debut in this sphere at Listowel 26 days ago (2m, good); hiked up 9lb for that and this is a deeper race but he can't be discounted.
runners.comment: Veteran, bolted up on Flat h'cap bow latest, up 9lb and this harder but can't discount

runners.name: Poetic Sound
runners.spotlight: Stayed on well to gain third career success off 8lb lower here on latest (1m6f, yielding); both previous runs at around this trip have not been his best so could be vulnerable upped in distance on quicker ground off career high mark.
runners.comment: Third career win over 1m6f here latest, career high mark, a bit to prove at this trip

runners.name: Carthusian
runners.spotlight: Showed promise in maidens but first three handicap runs have been pretty underwhelming; beaten 9l at Fairyhouse on latest (1m6f, good); 6lb wrong here; others appeal more.
runners.comment: Hasn't built on maiden promise, beaten 9l at F'house latest, 6lb wrong here

runners.name: Reverend Hubert
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose; signed off last season with a solid effort in the Cesarewitch at Newmarket (2m2f, gd-fm); may have needed the run over an inadequate 1m6f here last month; sharper now and moves up in trip; beaten in this last year off 8lb lower but respected nonetheless.
runners.comment: May have needed latest here over 1m6f, respected stepped back up in distance

runners.name: Happy Jacky
runners.spotlight: Veteran has notched up nine wins over jumps but is 1-9 on the Flat and hasn't won in this sphere since 2024; however, he was a close third in this last season off 1lb higher on good ground so he's not dismissed; 1lb wrong.
runners.comment: Veteran, close third in this last season on good ground, 1lb wrong here but not dismissed

runners.name: Hatim
runners.spotlight: Three-time winner at 1m4f-1m7f on testing ground in France; ground may be an issue on first start for new stable; 3lb wrong and best watched in new headgear combination.
runners.comment: Three wins in France on testing ground, conditions a worry on first run for new stable

runners.name: Highwind
runners.spotlight: Second runner for yard; sole win on Flat came over 1m on AW in 2023 in France; may have needed the run when weakening over 1m6f here (good) on latest last month; ran well over 18.5f in Ulster Cesarewitch last September off 1lb lower; chance stepped back up in trip.
runners.comment: May have needed latest, good run off 1lb lower in Ulster Cesarewitch last Sept', chance

runners.name: Cock And Bull
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose; 3-13 on the Flat for Michael Bell, all three wins over 1m6f on fast ground; has placed AW form at around this trip; modest form on easy ground over hurdles so far for this yard but return to quicker conditions on the Flat could revive; 2lb wrong.
runners.comment: Unsuitable ground over hurdles for this yard, could revive returned to quicker conditions
runners.owner: Sarah Dale & Wayne Dale & Ian Foster

runners.name: The Shunter
runners.spotlight: Dual-purpose veteran; useful in both codes and ran very well in a C&D Group 3 (heavy) when last sighted in November; majority of best form has come on ground with an ease so probably best watched on return from 236 days off.
runners.comment: Veteran, fine run in C&D Gr' 3 when last seen in November, may need this run after break

runners.name: The Mediator
runners.spotlight: Bumper, hurdle and Flat winner ran a solid race on return at Listowel and is a big player.
runners.comment: Bumper, hurdle and Flat winner ran a solid race on return at Listowel and is a player
runners.owner: Mrs Sheila O'Ryan & John F Doyle & Grant Leon

runners.name: San Andreas
runners.spotlight: Only two of 10 wins have been on turf but rated 80 so has to be respected in this grade.
runners.comment: Only two of 10 wins have been on turf but rated 80 so has to be respected

runners.name: Nancee Spain
runners.spotlight: Easy Roscommon winner last year and a solid fifth at Gowran last time; best form on soft.
runners.comment: Easy Roscommon winner last year and a solid fifth at Gowran last time; best on soft

runners.name: Garnetot
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; rated 100 over hurdles but has been poor on last two starts; not high on the list.
runners.comment: Rated 100 over hurdles but has been poor on last two starts; not high on the list; reserve

runners.name: Wackestone
runners.spotlight: Has shown ability without winning on the Flat and over hurdles; poor hurdle run last time, though.
runners.comment: Has shown ability without winning on the Flat and over hurdles; poor hurdle run last time

runners.name: Spinola Bay
runners.spotlight: Won twice over hurdles last winter but was below form in that sphere when last seen.
runners.comment: Won twice over hurdles last winter but was below form in that sphere when last seen

runners.name: Playtime
runners.spotlight: First reserve; Flat winner last year won over hurdles earlier this year; has the ability to go well.
runners.comment: Flat winner last year won over hurdles earlier this year; the ability to go well; reserve

runners.name: Limbocall
runners.spotlight: Well beaten over 1m on return at Limerick last time and has a bit to find here.
runners.comment: Well beaten over 1m on return at Limerick last time and has a bit to find here

runners.name: Red Hugh O'Donnell
runners.spotlight: Killarney maiden winner over 1m last year but was well beaten on return over that trip.
runners.comment: Killarney maiden winner over 1m last year but well beaten on return over that trip

runners.name: Western Model
runners.spotlight: Hurdle winner ran a solid race at Downpatrick last time; has a bit to find on Flat debut.
runners.comment: Hurdle winner ran a solid race at Downpatrick last time; a bit to find on Flat debut

runners.name: Catalani
runners.spotlight: Triple point winner was an easy hunter chase winner last time; this trip might be a bit sharp.
runners.comment: Triple point winner was an easy hunter chase winner last time; this trip might be sharp

runners.name: Way Down South
runners.spotlight: Point, hurdle and chase winner was poor over hurdles last time and has to rebound.
runners.comment: Point, hurdle and chase winner was poor over hurdles last time and has to rebound

runners.name: Whatyouwant
runners.spotlight: Kilbeggan h'cap hurdle winner last year was pulled-up next time; has a bit to find on return.
runners.comment: Kilbeggan h'cap hurdle winner last year was pulled-up next time; has a bit to find

runners.name: Banana Three
runners.spotlight: AW Flat winner was disappointing over hurdles when last seen over a year ago.
runners.comment: AW Flat winner was disappointing over hurdles when last seen over a year ago

runners.name: Elman
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; maiden in all codes was well beaten here over 1m on last Flat start.
runners.comment: Maiden in all codes was well beaten here over 1m on last Flat start; reserve

runners.name: Typical Thomas
runners.spotlight: Hurdle and chase winner ran respectably on sole Flat start; not a bad hurdle run last time; chance.
runners.comment: Hurdle and chase winner ran respectably on sole Flat start; not a bad hurdle run last time

races.race_name: Zyn In The Moment British EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Acting Lady
runners.spotlight: Second foal; half-sister to 6f 2yo winner Crimson Rose (RPR 87); dam French 5f/7f winner (incuding 2yo/Group 3; 108), half-sister to useful 7f 2yo winner Pacifica Pier, out of 7f 2yo Listed winning sister to US 7f Grade 2 winner True Quality; interesting newcomer from top stable.
runners.comment: Out of a French Group 3 winner; interesting newcomer from a top stable

runners.name: Sexy Chick
runners.spotlight: 160,000gns yearling; third foal; half-sister to 5f AW 2yo winner Mwaki (RPR 62); unraced dam closely related to 5f Group 2 winner Priceless, half-sister to smart 6f-1m winner Doctor Brown; could be against two useful fillies on her debut but still makes some appeal.
runners.comment: 160,000gns yearling; unraced dam closely related to 5f Group 2 winner Priceless

runners.name: War Banner
runners.spotlight: 230,000gns yearling; first foal; dam 5f 2yo winner (including Listed; RPR 101), closely related to 1m4f winner Sioux Princess, out of French 8.5f 2yo winning half-sister to Listed winners Power Elite (7f 2yo) and La Sylvia (6f); looks the part on paper.
runners.comment: 230,000gns yearling; out of a Listed 2yo winner; looks the part on paper

races.race_name: John Banks Group Front Runner Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Starlight Lass
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam 1m 2yo winner (RPR 90), half-sister to St Leger winner Logician; encountered some traffic before running on well to finish second at Yarmouth (7f, good to soft) 16 days ago; ought to be hard to beat.
runners.comment: Encountered traffic before running on well for second at Yarmouth; strong claims

runners.name: Yimmna
runners.spotlight: 650,000euros yearling; half-sister to a French Listed winner; dam half-sister to French 1m4f Group 1 winner Left Hand; showed her inexperience and finished over 3l behind Starlight Lass when seventh of 11 at Yarmouth (7f, good to soft) this month but has obvious scope for improvement.
runners.comment: 650,000euros yearling; behind Starlight Lass on debut but should improve

runners.name: Love Me Bae
runners.spotlight: Well beaten at triple-digit odds on both starts, the latter her seasonal/stable debut at Windsor (1m, good to soft) this month.
runners.comment: Well beaten at triple-digit odds on both starts

runners.name: Polka Blue
runners.spotlight: Has been threatening to get off the mark since the autumn and things have fallen right for her of late, following a 6f win at Leicester (good to firm) with an impressive one on Lingfield AW (7f); strong traveller who shouldn't mind the return to 6f; still has potential.
runners.comment: Fulfilled promise of late, winning over 6f at Leicester and 7f on AW; still has potential

runners.name: There's A Chance
runners.spotlight: Promising 3yo who made a successful start to her handicap career at Hamilton (good) last July and made it 2-2 in 6f turf handicaps when winning in good style at Haydock (soft) in May; a 5lb rise looks less of a worry than the switch in surface but she's going the right way.
runners.comment: Made it 2-2 in 6f turf handicaps when ready winner at Haydock latest; up 5lb; improving

runners.name: Brisk Symphony
runners.spotlight: Won 6f maiden and novice events (good to firm) as 2yo and had a difficult weights task when beaten under 6l in C&D Listed race on the Rowley course in October; two good efforts in 7f handicaps, on turf and AW, this year and likely to get the good pace needed now back at 6f; thereabouts.
runners.comment: Two 6f wins as 2yo; good efforts in 7f handicaps this year; needs good pace back at 6f

runners.name: Angel Shared
runners.spotlight: Front-running C&D hat-trick on good to firm last summer started with a win in this race; as good as ever in 5f events at Bath this year and a subsequent Listed defeat on good to soft at Haydock (28-1) can be glossed over; yard also won this in 2022 and she's likely to be on her game.
runners.comment: Won this race last year, followed by two more over C&D; as good as ever in 2026

runners.name: Shallow
runners.spotlight: Often leads; C&D winner last July; two narrow defeats from three starts since cheekpieces have been fitted, over 7f at Yarmouth and 6f at York (both on good); a very likely contender back over this C&D.
runners.comment: C&D winner last July; two close 2nds from three starts in cheekpieces; likely contender

runners.name: Lara Antipova
runners.spotlight: Confirmed the promise of her first two starts when dictating the pace to her advantage in a 6f maiden on Lingfield AW in April; open to improvement now handicapping back on turf but she refused to go in the stalls at the start of the month and there'll be no easy lead on a plate today.
runners.comment: Made all in 6f AW maiden latest; handicap debut but no easy lead this time
runners.owner: Moulton Racing And Mrs A Jordan

runners.name: Eskimo Pie
runners.spotlight: Maiden who acquitted herself well in some tough 5f races as 2yo, particularly when third in her group in the Group 2 Queen Mary at Royal Ascot; returned with a good third of six in a fillies' conditions race on Kempton AW (1m) in March but subsequent defeats in novice events haven't approached that form; goes handicapping over 6f with a query to answer.
runners.comment: Ran well in tough company as 2yo; good comeback but not as good since; handicap debut

runners.name: Powdering
runners.spotlight: Multiple wins include one over 7f on the Rowley course and she's also finished close up over 6f there; needs more than she's been showing of late though and this is her first run on the July course.
runners.comment: Multiple wins; needs more than she's been showing of late; first run on the July course

runners.name: Phantom Watch
runners.spotlight: No great progress in AW handicaps from last October to January but he found a powerful finish in his first turf run at this level when winning over C&D (good) last week; this could take more winning under a penalty (goes up the same amount in future) but he's open to improvement.
runners.comment: No progress on AW but he came clear in good style over C&D latest; could be more to come

runners.name: Daretobedifferent
runners.spotlight: All races at up to 7f until finishing close up over 1m on the Rowley course in May, then held off an in-form rival at Nottingham (also on good ground); seemed to find 1m1f on soft a shade too much at Lingfield since but her claims are more solid than most now back at 1m.
runners.comment: Stretched by 1m1f on soft latest but two good runs over 1m on good previously; thereabouts

runners.name: Khuskhas
runners.spotlight: 33-1 winner of 6f maiden at Yarmouth as 2yo but she's found handicaps rather hard work since and was well back when upped to 1m on the Rowley course in May on her second run for this yard; needs so much more.
runners.comment: Won 6f maiden as 2yo but held in handicaps since, including upped to 1m latest

runners.name: Just Typical
runners.spotlight: Rarely seen on turf and below best since a close call on AW before Christmas but he had swamped his rivals at Redcar (1m, good) last September and was seriously hampered on the Rowley course after; weighted to go well and respected; usual aids missing but two wins minus headgear in 2025.
runners.comment: Usually races on AW but impressive at Redcar (1m) last September; can go well back on turf

runners.name: Poke The Bear
runners.spotlight: Three AW wins at 1m and 7f in midwinter and he wasn't too far off his best at Wolverhampton recently but his two turf runs, most recently at Yarmouth in May, leave him with some questions hanging.
runners.comment: Three AW wins at 1m/7f in midwinter; well beaten in just two turf runs, the latest in May

runners.name: Big Alex Walmsley
runners.spotlight: Improved form when close up in a 7f Redcar maiden on his third qualifying run but he hasn't yet looked well treated in handicaps, or proved he stays 1m; others have less to prove.
runners.comment: Not yet looked well treated in handicaps and has stamina to prove back over 1m

runners.name: Norcross Brow
runners.spotlight: 0-7 but he's shown enough at 1m on the AW to think there's a race in him and perhaps the first-time hood (now discarded) allied to good to soft ground combined against him back on turf at Ripon in April; sold for 8,000gns since; worth a look on debut for new yard.
runners.comment: Maiden; looks to have a race in him on AW form; worth a look on yard debut

runners.name: Hour By Hour
runners.spotlight: All wins at 6f on ground ranging from good to soft to heavy, the last three in 2024; well below best on final starts for previous trainer and he's beaten a total of one rival in two AW runs (1m) for this one in 2026; has a lot to prove.
runners.comment: All wins at 6f on good to soft/slower; beaten long way in two 1m starts on AW for new yard

runners.name: Double Time
runners.spotlight: Front-runs; acts on good to firm ground but his turf wins (both over 1m) have come on good to soft and soft and he's been off since a lesser effort on Southwell AW in January; tends to need the run after a break and conditions aren't ideal.
runners.comment: Both turf wins (1m) on good to soft and soft; off since January; likely to need the run

runners.name: Rockafeller Skank
runners.spotlight: Ran so moderately in qualifying starts that he had an opening mark of 25 but he's been supercharged in classified events of late, winning four in nine days (1m-1m2f; soft and good); has more to do under a big penalty (1lb well in) but he may well improve further; however, he was taken out of a recent intended race on good to firm so there is a surface query.
runners.comment: Supercharged in classifieds, winning 4 in 9 days; this is harder but he's improving

runners.name: Booziebrunch
runners.spotlight: Successful in 1m handicap on AW as 2yo but he ran through the options over that trip; seemed to stay this trip on Lingfield AW last time, coming from off the pace to take third of six behind one who followed up and this might end by not being a true test of stamina anyway; respected.
runners.comment: Ran through the options over 1m; seemed to stay 1m2f on AW latest; likely to go well

runners.name: Jodhpur Blue
runners.spotlight: Sole win in 7f AW maiden as 3yo; very understated in early handicaps for this yard but he's been involved in three tight finishes at 1m3f and 1m2f on AW in 2026 and the step up to 2m in March looked more of an experiment than a plan; acts on turf and has claims back at 1m2f.
runners.comment: Sole win at 7f on AW as 3yo; three close calls on sand in 2026; not ruled out back on turf

runners.name: Maith Mar Or
runners.spotlight: From a good middle-distance family but there were definite signs of ability over 1m as a 3yo, as on her latest turf start when showing the right spirit in fifth of 11 at Nottingham (first two now have marks in the high 80s); of interest up in trip but entitled to need this belated return.
runners.comment: Showed ability over 1m as 3yo; bred to be suited by 1m2f; handicap debut; belated return

runners.name: Hardy's Hero
runners.spotlight: Won 6f maiden and novice events as 2yo; two good efforts over 7f this year, doing well when attempting to give weight to a big improver on the other Newmarket course (good) and no disgrace in a Lingfield defeat next time when having to be switched; solid chance.
runners.comment: Both wins at 6f as 2yo; good 2nd to an improver on return; excuse latest; solid chance

runners.name: Marengo Storm
runners.spotlight: All three runs at 6f; beat a subsequent maiden winner in good style on Kempton AW on second start then was awkwardly positioned before finishing a never-nearer runner-up at Windsor (good to firm) in May; failed to enter the stalls since; capable of better off his current mark if seeing out 7f.
runners.comment: All 3 runs at 6f, impressive on AW in May, then hampered at Windsor; can do better

runners.name: Amorim
runners.spotlight: Has led; won 6f Windsor novice as 2yo, also in the frame in Listed events; found little when upped to 7f on Newbury comeback but finished close up back over 6f at Windsor recently in new aids (tongue-tie retained, blinkers discarded); stamina still to be proved over 7f but can't rule out.
runners.comment: Found little on sole 7f start, on comeback; close up over 6f since; stamina query
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Dereham
runners.spotlight: 10yo whose last three wins came in this race (2022, 2023 and 2025) and it's now named after him; did it in good style from the back last year (good to firm) and is now 3lb higher; made a satisfactory return to action at Yarmouth last month and the chief concern today is probably whether there will be enough pace in the race for his stamina to kick in sufficiently.
runners.comment: Last three wins came in this race (2022, 2023 and 2025); concern about pace in the race

runners.name: Vilaine Verlaine
runners.spotlight: Dual 1m2f AW winner in 2024; upped from 1m4f to 2m under Alicia Perkins on Kempton AW 16 days ago and gave a positive response, as she travelled smoothly in rear but met some traffic before running on into second of eight; has some turf form (not this year) and could have more to offer as a stayer.
runners.comment: Upped from 1m4f to 2m on Kempton AW 16 days ago and ran on into second of eight

runners.name: Prince Quattro
runners.spotlight: Four-time hurdles winner whose first Flat success came on Southwell AW (1m6f) in January; has kept his form pretty well, including over 2m, and is considered seriously in this small field despite having had just two races (2025) on Flat turf.
runners.comment: Has kept his form pretty well on AW, including over 2m, and is considered seriously
runners.owner: Ursa Major Ltd & Hicky Parmar Racing

runners.name: Betelgeuse
runners.spotlight: Ran away with a Southwell handicap (1m6f, AW) last October; second twice there (2m/1m6f) this April and he wasn't beaten far when making the running on good to firm at Yarmouth (1m6f) eight days ago, albeit coming last in a three-runner race; chance if he takes to the first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Made the running in three-runner race latest; chance if taking to the first-time headgear

runners.name: Now The Eagle
runners.spotlight: Ex-French 4yo who is 1-7 for current connections, easily best of those outings all from off the pace over 2m on Kempton AW; five-day break was blamed when he tailed off on turf (2m, good to firm) on latest outing, but that still leaves him with something to prove.
runners.comment: Five-day break was blamed when he tailed off on turf (2m, good to firm) on latest outing

runners.name: Belle Amie
runners.spotlight: Broke her duck at first attempt for this yard, in a 1m4f classified in February; returned to turf with a creditable third in handicap at Windsor (11.5f, good to firm) before winning one comfortably from midfield at Bath (11.5f, good) 13 days ago; up 5lb but she's now on a bit of a roll.
runners.comment: Won comfortably at Bath (11.5f, good) 13 days ago; up 5lb but she's now on a bit of a roll

runners.name: Seventy
runners.spotlight: Building up a solid record for this yard, including one win on AW (this February) and two at Windsor (May and June); the latest win was in tidy style over almost this far 11 days ago and, although not well-in under his penalty, he has major claims again.
runners.comment: Good record for this yard; latest win was in tidy style over almost this far 11 days ago

runners.name: Nuptown Girl
runners.spotlight: 0-14 but achieved some minor honours over 1m-1m4f on AW this year and another when staying-on fourth of nine at Bath (1m2f, good) on latest start; arguably has a bit to prove over this far.
runners.comment: 0-14; staying-on fourth at Bath (1m2f) latest; arguably has a bit to prove over this far

runners.name: Market House
runners.spotlight: Made hay initially for this yard last March-June; pretty consistent this year but with only the one win (with first-time visor over hurdles in April) and he may again have to settle for minor honours.
runners.comment: Pretty consistent this year but with only the one win (with first-time visor over hurdles)

runners.name: Green Team
runners.spotlight: Third start for new yard when winning from the front at Wolverhampton (1m4f amateurs, AW) four weeks ago on latest outing; back up 4lb; chief concern is that it's over two years since he raced on firmer than good and he was well beaten on last four attempts on it; last July's turf win was on good to soft.
runners.comment: Won from the front at Wolverhampton (1m4f amateurs, AW) latest; good to firm is a concern

runners.name: Le Gentleman
runners.spotlight: Ex-French 5yo who was left clear at final fence to win comfortably by 11l over C&D (good) last month, his third start in Britain; 7lb higher today but open to further progress and needs to be taken very seriously.
runners.comment: Left clear to win comfortably by 11l over C&D last month and remains unexposed in Britain
runners.owner: The North West National Hunt Racing Club

runners.name: Country Park
runners.spotlight: Never really got going during light campaign over hurdles last season but has some fairly useful form on his CV and made quite encouraging start to chasing career when fourth at Worcester (2m4f, good) last month; can't be ignored.
runners.comment: Made fairly encouraging chase debut last month and can't be ignored

runners.name: My Noble Lord
runners.spotlight: Seemingly boosted by change of headgear when winning by 6l over C&D (good) in first-time blinkers in April; obvious contender if judged on that evidence but ran a lacklustre race at Aintree last month and it's hard to tell whether he'll be in the right mood in first-time visor today.
runners.comment: C&D winner in new blinkers in April; lacklustre last time; now tried in a visor

runners.name: Presentandcounting
runners.spotlight: Good-ground specialist with nine chase wins on his CV; long in the tooth now but returned from break with two respectable runs this spring and has edged his way back down to a good mark; no surprise if he makes a bold bid from the front.
runners.comment: Both runs this year were respectable and his current mark is workable; can make bold bid

runners.name: Slaney Opera
runners.spotlight: Not at best when third of four in first-time blinkers (retained) last month but started off for Nick Scholfield with two good efforts in cheekpieces (one over C&D) and was recently given a wind op; big player if quickly back on song.
runners.comment: Not at best last time but started off for this stable with two good efforts (one over C&D)

runners.name: Henry Box Brown
runners.spotlight: Returned from break with two below-par runs towards the end of last season and suffered mid-race mishap on latest outing this month but he has slipped to a good mark and can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Yet to hit top gear this year but has slipped to a good mark; can't be ruled out

runners.name: Radharc Na Slaine
runners.spotlight: Came good with narrow win off lowly mark at Stratford (2m6f, good; first-time cheekpieces) in September but last month's reappearance run was disappointing; given another wind op (his second) since; others are more persuasive.
runners.comment: Gained narrow win at Stratford in September but last month's return was disappointing

runners.name: Friends Don't Ask
runners.spotlight: Four-time chase winner; returned from break with three good efforts this spring, including a C&D third (good), but looked very laboured when well beaten at Southwell 17 days ago; needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Placed three times this spring but needs to rebound from a laboured effort 17 days ago

runners.name: Rockvale
runners.spotlight: Placed for first time under rules when clear second to a next-time-out winner at Uttoxeter (2m5f, good) last month but was pulled up (bled from the nose) on latest outing; back up in trip with tongue-tie added here; not an easy one to predict.
runners.comment: Second to a next-time-out winner two starts ago but pulled up since; hard to predict

runners.name: Est Illic
runners.spotlight: Won three-runner point in April but struggled when back in a handicap chase three weeks ago, and this veteran needs to respond very positively to a change of headgear (usually wears cheekpieces).
runners.comment: Won small-field point in April but struggled when back in a handicap chase this month

runners.name: Glenmalure Flyer
runners.spotlight: 0-10 over hurdles when based in Ireland and it was her 11th run in a maiden point there when prevailing by a neck on good ground last month; now with top stable for this chasing debut, though, having recently changed hands for £5,000.
runners.comment: Eventually off the mark in Irish points last month; now with top stable for chase debut

runners.name: Dazzlelikethis
runners.spotlight: Launched chasing career with two heavy defeats in the autumn (3m/2m4f) but returns to racing under rules after winning good-ground maiden point by 11l last month; interesting to see what the market makes of her.
runners.comment: Well beaten in both handicap chases in the autumn but won a recent point by 11l

runners.name: Vinnie O'Neil
runners.spotlight: Unplaced all seven hurdle runs, having been well beaten in two 2m3f handicaps here this spring; needs combination of new trip and switch to fences to prompt significant improvement.
runners.comment: Unplaced all seven hurdle starts; up in trip for chase debut; improvement needed

runners.name: Shuile Warrior
runners.spotlight: Won a point last year and is still unexposed under rules but was pulled up over C&D on handicap/chase debut in April; difficult to enthuse over.
runners.comment: Still unexposed under rules but was pulled up over C&D on handicap/chase debut in April

races.race_name: Wrexham Lager Mares' Maiden Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Colegram
runners.spotlight: Won two small-field Irish points this spring (both on soft) and made pretty good start to rules career when third at Newton Abbot (2m1f, good) a fortnight ago; ought to be suited by this move back up in trip; good chance.
runners.comment: Dual point winner; good start to rules career when third at Newton Abbot this month

runners.name: Mirabello
runners.spotlight: Improved with experience in bumpers, most recently when second to well-backed newcomer at Newton Abbot (good) six weeks ago; that form probably isn't anything to get too excited about but she's likely to be suited by today's longer trip and should have a future over jumps.
runners.comment: Second in Newton Abbot bumper last month; should have a future over jumps

runners.name: Moonshine River
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to useful jumper Johnson's Blue; placed on soft/heavy ground in the last three of her six points, and subsequently changed hands for £10,000; makes rules debut after 138-day break and encounters much quicker conditions but good market support will make her of interest.
runners.comment: Placed in three slow-ground Irish points in the winter; market helpful on rules debut

runners.name: Little Twig
runners.spotlight: Out of an unraced sister to high-class chaser Nacarat; bettered low-key Irish point debut when third of 12 last month (good to yielding), form that was boosted when runner-up won a recent Hexham bumper; subsequently bought for £19,000; interesting to see how she fares on rules debut.
runners.comment: Bought for £19,000 after last month's Irish point third; makes rules debut today

runners.name: Brenda Lady
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten on first two hurdling starts (2m/2m5f) and not the most obvious answer here.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten on first two hurdling starts (2m/2m5f) and not the most obvious answer here

runners.name: Pearl Knot
runners.spotlight: Third foal; half-sister to low-grade chase winner Pearl Island; dam 2m6f-3m hurdle winner, sister to 3m-3m5f chase winner Bretney, closely related to useful 2m4f-4m hurdle/chase winner Captain Cattistock; stoutly bred filly; wouldn't need to be anything special to get competitive on debut.
runners.comment: Stoutly bred filly; wouldn't need to be anything special to get competitive on debut

runners.name: Psychologicaldrama
runners.spotlight: Gave it a good shot from the front when third of five in junior hurdle at Leicester (1m7f, soft) in November but two lesser efforts for Ryan Potter followed; now with new stable (same owners); improvement required.
runners.comment: Showed promise on debut for Ryan Potter but two lesser efforts followed; new yard

runners.name: Crystal Glance
runners.spotlight: Returned from six-month break with career-best performance in May, winning readily by 5l over C&D (good), and is now 5-13 over hurdles; recent 8b rise asks a question of her but she's evidently in very good nick.
runners.comment: Returned from six-month break with career-best performance over C&D last month; up 8b

runners.name: Il Va De Soi
runners.spotlight: Bounced back to form in refitted cheekpieces for Harry Derham last month, winning twice at Warwick (2m5f, good); subsequently changed hands for £12,000; remains well handicapped on older form.
runners.comment: Signed off for Harry Derham with two Warwick wins last month; now with Donald McCain

runners.name: Supreme Yeats
runners.spotlight: Back in good form in refitted cheepieces this spring, winning twice at Newton Abbot (3m2f, good) before very respectable third at Hexham (2m7f, good); not sure to be ideally suited by today's drop back in trip or the switch back to blinkers but still considered.
runners.comment: Back in good form this spring, albeit over longer trips; considered
runners.owner: The Old Stag Racing Sy Partnership

runners.name: Summergrounds
runners.spotlight: Late-maturing 10yo who won twice last spring and maintained progress with some good runs in defeat in second half of last year; may not have been suited by soft ground when pulled up on latest outing; not out of this.
runners.comment: Made good progress in 2025 and had possible ground excuse when pulled up on soft in May

races.race_name: Rachel Jones Memorial Novices' Handicap Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Centurion's Sister
runners.spotlight: In the frame all five hurdling starts, most recently when second in Cartmel novice (2m1f, good) last month, after a break; ought to be very competitive again on this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Good second in Cartmel novice last month; can be very competitive again on handicap debut

runners.name: Evening Tess
runners.spotlight: Close second on last month's handicap debut at Newton Abbot (2m2f, good) and bettered that performance when narrow winner of Fontwell novice (2m3f, good) 16 days ago; should be okay back at this shorter trip and probably still has potential.
runners.comment: Close second on handicap debut and has since won a novice; probably still has potential

runners.name: Into Battle
runners.spotlight: Fairly useful on the Flat when based with Ed Walker; well beaten on stable/hurdle debut in January but his next two runs were much more encouraging, and there is still hope that more of his Flat ability can be transferred to this discipline.
runners.comment: Fairly useful on Flat; has shown clear promise over hurdles; interesting on handicap debut

runners.name: American Empire
runners.spotlight: Kept on for third of 13 in Hereford maiden (2m, good) in April but was below that form when well-held third of six over that C&D on last month's handicap debut; needs help from the new tongue-tie here.
runners.comment: Placed in a maiden in April but below that form when well-held third on handicap debut

runners.name: Prettylady
runners.spotlight: 2-2 since switched to handicaps, the latter win gained at Southwell (2m, good) this month; did that with quite a bit more in hand than 1l-margin might suggest and could still be well treated after latest 8lb rise.
runners.comment: 2-2 since switched to handicaps and could still be well treated after latest 8lb rise

races.race_name: Tom Jones Live At Chester Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Golden Glance
