runners.spotlight: Out of form during light campaign last season but cashed in on much-reduced mark at Southwell (2m4f, good) this month; not yet proven over this longer trip but recent 4lb rise ought to be manageable; respected.
runners.comment: Cashed in on much-reduced mark this month (2m4f); 4lb rise ought to be manageable

runners.name: Rock Hurley
runners.spotlight: Suffered alarming dip in form after 2m3f course win (good to soft) last April but ran well from the front and was seemingly back in good nick when third over that trip here (good) last month; contender if able to build on that effort over this new trip.
runners.comment: Seemingly back in good nick when third here (2m3f) last month; tries a new trip today

runners.name: Athena's Dream
runners.spotlight: 0-6 over hurdles but looked suited by step up to 3m when second under Megan Bevan at Ludlow (good; made last-flight error) last month, form that was recently given a good boost by the third; high on the list.
runners.comment: Evidently well suited by 3m when runner-up at Ludlow last month; high on the list

runners.name: The Kings Soldier
runners.spotlight: Ran quite well when third of seven (albeit well held in the end) on recent chasing debut at Hexham (3m, good) but is now 0-9 under all codes; 2lb wrong for this return to hurding; needs to find something extra.
runners.comment: Ran quite well on recent chasing debut but needs to find something extra on hurdle return

runners.name: Roxboro River
runners.spotlight: Hinted at possible return to form when fourth at Stratford (2m6f, good to firm) last month and is now 2lb lower than for last summer's win over that C&D but he was soundly beaten at Market Rasen (2m4f) a fortnight ago and now has questions to answer again.
runners.comment: Penultimate run hinted at possible return to form; well held since, though

runners.name: Collingham
runners.spotlight: Won two claiming hurdes for Archie Watson (1m7f/2m) last spring but was 0-6 in handicaps for current stable afterwards; absent since heavy defeat over 2m3f in November and this reappearance run is his first venture beyond 2m5f; not the percentage call.
runners.comment: Absent since heavy defeat over 2m3f in November; first venture beyond 2m5f today

runners.name: Innisfree Lass
runners.spotlight: Gained all three hurdle wins for Stuart Edmonds over 3m on heavy and soft ground at Southwell, the latest in February; changed hands for 2,400gns in April and now makes stable debut after 104-day break; tongue tied for first time; may find the ground too lively.
runners.comment: Gained all three wins for previous yard on slow ground at Southwell; makes stable debut

runners.name: Jay Bee Whiskey
runners.spotlight: Seemed to respond well to first-time cheekpieces when second in a small-field point in April but has not raced under rules since 70l defeat on handicap hurdle debut in November 2024 (2m7f); has tongue-tie refitted here; others have more persuasive profiles.
runners.comment: Second in point in April; not seen under rules since 70l defeat on handicap debut in 2024

runners.name: Rb Yas Sir (Usa)
runners.spotlight: Won a very competitive staying handicap in Abu Dhabi at the end of last year and wasn't disgraced in two Group One races there this year; made a winning start here at Wolverhampton where he had a fitness edge and he beat two of today's rivals; track and trip should suit and despite giving weight all round he's the one to beat. (R28 W5 P7) (OR 104)

runners.name: Upstart Crow (GB)
runners.spotlight: A real improver for this yard only once finishing outside of the first three in fifteen starts and that came in Group One company; tas finished behind RB Yas Sir and Al Sharid (twice) this season but is better off with both at the weights this time around; can't rule out. (R18 W6 P8) (OR 92)

runners.name: Al Sharid (Fr)
runners.spotlight: Was flattered to get so close to RB Yas Sir on his debut at Wolverhampton; put that experience to good use when winning at Bath on his handicap debut under this rider last month; he was value for more than the winning margin that day and he's open to lots of improvement stepping up in trip; strong claims. (R2 W1 P1) (OR 90)

runners.name: Izenterisky (GB)
runners.spotlight: Defied an absence of five years to make a winning handicap debut at Chester two years ago and managed to win over this distance at Windsor last year; this is tougher, but he gets lots of weight all round; can't be discounted. (R5 W3 P1) (OR 82)
